item.title: 什么是单翼飞机 | what is a monoplane
item.description:
历史

单翼飞机是一种固定的单翼飞机。 单翼飞机设计一直是多翼飞机的通用配置，因为相邻机翼之间的气流干扰会降低效率。 第一架单翼飞机由航空先驱和罗马尼亚发明家 Trajan Vuia 建造，他建造了一架单翼飞机，于 1906 年 3 月 18 日飞行了 40 英尺。

单翼飞机的构成在世界大战期间证明了自己的绝对性，从那以后，除了特殊动机外，单翼飞机已经完全取代了双翼飞机。 它是最简单的构造。 然而，在飞行的早期，这些优势被它们更大的重量所抵消。 单翼飞机从根本上说是所有飞机机翼配置中效率最高和阻力最低的，但机动性较低，因此直到 1930 年代才相对罕见。 从那时起，单翼机就成了固定翼飞机最常见的形式。

特征
支持和重量

单翼飞机的结构效率在悬臂机翼中实现得最好，它在内部承受所有结构力。 尽管如此，要以实际速度飞行，机翼必须做得很好，这需要大量的结构来使其足够坚固和坚固。

外部刷新可用于提高结构能力并最大程度地减少重量和成本。 对于给定尺寸的机翼，减重允许它飞得更慢，并且使用更低功率和更有效的发动机更稳定。 在此基础上，所有处于研发阶段的单翼机翼都得到了加强，而且大部分都一直持续到 1930 年代初。 然而，暴露的支柱或金属丝会产生过多的阻力并降低空气动力学效率和最大速度。

高速和远程配置往往是真正的悬臂，而低速短程类型经常被赋予能量。

机翼位置

除了机翼布置的常见变化（例如尾部位置和支撑的使用）之外，单翼飞机类型之间的主要区别在于机翼直立安装在机身上的位置。

低的

低翼位于机身底部或附近。

将机翼布置得较低可以让向上看到良好​​的视野，并将中央机身从翼梁传送带中解放出来。 最小化钟摆稳定性，使飞机更具机动性，就像在喷火战斗机上一样，但重视稳定性而不是机动性的飞机可能需要一些上反角。

低翼位置的一个特点是其重要的地面效应，使飞机有可能在着陆前漂浮很长一段距离。 相互地，这种地面效应允许更短的起飞时间。

中翼

在中翼配置中，机翼正好安装在飞机的中线，机身高度的一半。 执行的翼梁结构可以最大限度地减少其重心附近所需的机身尺寸，而那里通常是最需要空间的地方。

肩膀

肩翼是一种机翼固定在机身顶部附近但不在最顶部的布置。 之所以这样称呼，是因为它装配在机身的“肩部”，比飞行员的肩部还大。 肩翼和高翼给出了一些特征，具体来说，它们支持下垂的机身，该机身不喜欢机翼上反角以获得稳定性，并且通过与低翼的区别，肩翼的有限地面效应最大限度地减少了着陆时的漂浮。

整理到低翼、肩翼和高翼设计可扩大多引擎飞机的螺旋桨间隙。 在大型飞机上，肩翼和高翼在实际应用中存在微小差异，但在优质飞机上，这种布置非常出色，因为它为飞行员提供了更高水平的能见度。 在一架优质飞机上，肩翼可能需要前掠以保持正确的重心。

高的

高机翼安装在机身的上表面或上方。 它与肩翼有很多优点和缺点，但在一架好飞机上，高翼向上的能见度很差。 在优良的飞机上，机翼通常布置在机舱上方，使翼梁流过乘员的头部，使机翼在前后情况下处于完美位置。 高翼布置的一个优点是机身更靠近地面，这便于货物装载，特别是对于带有后机身货物通道的飞机。 军用货运飞机大多采用高翼设计，带有后部货运通道。

阳伞

阳伞翼单翼飞机是一种飞机设计，其中机翼不直接连接到机身，而是固定在机身上方，由 Cabane 支柱或吊架辅助。 可以通过从机身侧面扩大的支柱或金属丝提供过度刺激。

第一架遮阳伞单翼飞机是肩翼单翼飞机的改造，因为在机身上方建造肩扛式机翼显着提高了向下的能见度，这对于检查角色很有用，就像在飞机上广泛使用的那样。 遮阳伞机翼经过精心设计，具有良好的飞行员视野，并被一些战斗机采用。

遮阳伞翼也被分配到发动机的高安装点，在战后时期，飞艇需要这种翼，因为飞艇需要将螺旋桨举起，使其远离水花。

与双翼飞机相比，阳伞翼具有更少的模拟和更低的阻力。 它仍然是两栖动物和小型人造手动和超精细飞机的需求安排。

大多数军用飞机都是单翼飞机，从那以后所有飞机实际上都是单翼飞机，不包括一些专业品种。

喷气和火箭发动机是更强大的高速现代飞机，主要是超音速飞机，具有单翼飞机结构。

单翼飞机和双翼飞机有什么区别？

单翼飞机是一种只有一个主翼的飞机，而双翼飞机有两个主翼，一个在另一个上面。 单翼飞机和双翼飞机之间的一些主要区别包括：

空气动力学效率：单翼飞机通常比双翼飞机具有更高的空气动力学效率，这意味着它们可以实现更高的速度和更远的航程。 这是因为单翼飞机的单翼产生的阻力小于双翼飞机的两个机翼。

阅读更多



原文:

History

A monoplane is a fixed, one-wing aircraft. The monoplane aircraft design has been a universal configuration of multiplane because airflow interference in between the adjacent wings reduces the efficiency. The first monoplane aircraft was constructed by an aviation pioneer and Romanian inventor Trajan Vuia, who constructed a Monoplane aircraft, which flew 40 feet on March 18, 1906.

In 1907, French aviator Louis Bleriot constructed a monoplane and flew it across the Atlantic ocean two years later. Monoplane composition proved itself absolute during the world war, and ever since then, the monoplane has completely replaced the biplane except for special motives. It is the simplest to construct. However, during the early years of flight, these advantages were offset by their greater weight. A monoplane has fundamentally the highest efficiency and lowest drag of any aircraft wing configuration and lowers mobility, making it correspondingly rare until the 1930s. Since then, the monoplane has been the most familiar form for a fixed-wing aircraft.

Characteristics
Support and weight

The structural efficiency of the monoplane is best achieved in the cantilever wing, which carries all structural forces internally. Still, to fly at in effect speeds the wing must be made fine, which preferred massive construction to make it strong and rigid enough.

External refreshing can be used to improve structural ability and minimize weight and cost. For a wing of given dimensions, the mass deduction allows it to fly slower, and steady with a lower-powered and more effective engine. For this basis, all monoplane wings in the developer stage were strengthened and most were up until the early 1930s. However, the revealed struts or wires create excess drag and reduce aerodynamic efficiency and maximum speed.  

High-speed and long-range configurations tend to be true cantilevers, while low-speed short-range types are frequently given energizing.

Wing Position

On the side of the common variety in wing arrangements such as tail position and use of bracing, the main difference between types of monoplane is where the wing is mounted upright on the fuselage. 

Low

A low wing is situated on or near the basis of the fuselage.

Arranging the wing low allows good visuality upwards and frees the central fuselage from the wing spar convey through. Minimizing pendulum stability, makes the aircraft more mobility, as on the spitfire but aircraft that value stability over mobility may need some dihedral. 

A characteristic of the low-wing position is its important ground effect, giving the plane a likelihood to float a long way before landing.  Mutually, this ground effect allows shorter takeoffs.

Mid Wing

 In a Mid wing configuration, the wing is mounted exactly at the midline of the plane, at half of the height of the fuselage. The executed spar formation can minimize the required fuselage size near its center of gravity, where space is frequently in most need.

Shoulder

A shoulder wing is an arrangement whereby the wing is fixed near the top of the fuselage but not on the very top. It is so-called because it is assembled on the ‘shoulder’ of the fuselage, greater than on the pilot's shoulder. Shoulder wings and high wings give out some features, specifically, they assist a pendulous fuselage that prefers no wing dihedral for stability and, by differentiation with a low wing, a shoulder wing's finite ground effect minimizes float on landing.

Collate to a low wing, shoulder wing and high wing designs give expand propeller clearance on multi-engine aircraft.  On a massive aircraft, there is a tiny practical dissimilarity between a shoulder wing and a high wing but on fine aircraft, the arrangement is outstanding because it provides higher level visibility to the pilot. On a fine aircraft, the shoulder wing may require to be swept forward to retain the correct center of gravity.  

High

A high wing is mounted on the upper surface or above the top of the fuselage. It has many advantages and disadvantages with the shoulder wing, but on a fine aircraft, the high wing has bad upwards visibility. On fine aircraft, the wing is usually arranged above the cabin, so that the wing spar flows over the occupant's heads, leaving the wing in the perfect position in the front and rear situation.  An advantage of the high-wing arrangement is that the fuselage is nearer to the ground which eases cargo loading, particularly for aircraft with a rear fuselage cargo gateway. Military cargo aircraft are mostly high-wing designs with a rear cargo gateway.  

Parasol

A parasol wing monoplane is an aircraft design in which the wings are not directly attached to the fuselage but held above it, assist by either Cabane struts or pylon. Excess stimulation may be provided by struts or wires enlarged from the fuselage sides.

The first parasol monoplanes were a transformation of shoulder wing monoplanes since constructing a shoulder-mounted wing above the fuselage significantly enhances visibility downwards, which was useful for inspection roles, as with the widely used in aircraft planes.  The parasol wing authorizes for a well-planned design with fine pilot visibility and was acquired for some fighter planes.

A parasol wing was also assigned a high mounting point for engines and during the post-war period was in demand on flying boats, which needed to lift the propellers clear of spray.  

Collate to a biplane, a parasol wing has less simulation and lower drag. It remains a demand arrangement for amphibians and small man-made manual and ultra-fine aircraft.

Most military aircraft were monoplanes, as have been effectively all aircraft since, excluding a few specialist varieties.

Jet and rocket engines are more powerful and high-speed modern aircraft, chiefly supersonic variety, have monoplane aircraft structures.

What is the difference between a monoplane and an biplane?

A monoplane is a type of aircraft with a single main wing, while a biplane has two main wings, one above the other. Some key differences between monoplanes and biplanes include:

Aerodynamic efficiency: Monoplanes are typically more aerodynamically efficient than biplanes, which means they can achieve higher speeds and have a longer range. This is because the single wing of a monoplane produces less drag than the two wings of a biplane.

item.title: Software Engineering Entrepreneurship » 第 5 期 » 使用 Google Cloud 启动并运行 | Software Engineering Entrepreneurship » Issue 5 » Up and running with Google Cloud
item.description:
拥有一个可促进快速失败业务的弹性、可扩展系统的回报完全是本系列的重点。

学习 Google Cloud 一直是并将继续是一个跨越数月的漫长旅程。 技术发展迅速； 随之而来的是，数以千计的支持文档和资源在网络和 Google 的域中传播开来。 可用于帮助您的大量内容是阻碍您前进的最大因素。

为了应对解密 Google Cloud 文档这一庞大任务，您可以采取先让自己沉浸在技术中，然后再进行优化的方法。 这意味着了解您知道将使用哪些产品，然后真正进入并与这些产品进行交互。

系统设计可视化

选项 A：许多 VM 以实现高容错

当采用头先入为主的方法学习 GCP 时，我很容易努力构建我认为性能最高和容错能力最强的系统。 将有一个完全独立于其自己的机器的暂存环境，该环境将与生产分开扩展。 此外，如果任何单个组件发生故障，无论是暂存、生产、数据库还是应用程序，网络的其余部分仍有很大机会仍然可用。

然而，这种争取最佳系统的心态本身动摇的地方是成本和未使用的资源。 对于每运行一个额外的虚拟机，您每月需要支付 40-100 美元以上的费用。 在你发展的初始阶段，这对个人来说是不切实际的。 这里的例外情况是，如果您正在与一个快速移动的团队合作，并且您的启动阶段离未来不远。

选项 B：共享 VM 以降低成本

您可以在下方看到一个备用基础架构，该基础架构演示了如何在使用更少资源的情况下实现相同的结果。 这里的许多缺点之一是您在 GKE 集群和数据库的暂存和生产之间共享单点故障。 如果一个去，他们两个都去。 这里的巨大好处是您在最初构建应用程序时节省了大量 VM 成本。

推荐📌

使用共享虚拟机，然后在启动前迁移到许多虚拟机。 此方法要求您维护用于设置基础架构的强大文档。 您将需要回顾您使用云产品的经验，以最大限度地减少您在接近启动应用程序时花费的时间。 通过采用这种方法，您可以在种子前/开发阶段每月为自己节省数百美元。

接下来的是...

Software Engineering Entrepreneurship » Issue 6 » Development environments to live and breathe by Software Engineering Entrepreneurship » 第 6 期 » 赖以生存和呼吸的开发环境
Feb. 16th - 23rd 2月16日-23日



原文:

...[your leading cloud provider] combined with other edge technologies (DNS, API gateways, etc.) are constantly evolving and have steep learning curves. The payoff of having a resilient, scalable system that promotes fail-fast business is entirely what this series will focus on.

Learning Google Cloud has been and continues to be a long journey spanning over many months. The technology evolves quickly; Along with it thousands of supporting documents and resources have spread across the web and Google's domain. The sheer amount of content available to help you doubles as the largest factor capable of holding you back.

To counter this massive task of deciphering Google Cloud docs, you can take the approach of immersing yourself in the technology first and then optimizing later. This means having an understanding of which products you know you will use, and then literally going in and interacting with those products.

System design visualizations

Option A: Many VMs for high fault tolerance

When taking the head-first approach to learning GCP it was easy for me to strive to build the system that I figured would be the most performant and fault tolerant. There would be a staging environment isolated entirely to its own machines which would scale separately from production. Moreover, if any single component failed, be it staging, production, database or application, the rest of the network would have a good chance of still being available.

Where this mentality of striving for the optimal system falters itself however is the cost and unused resources. For each extra VM you operate you're paying $40-100+/month. During your initial stages of development this is not practical for individuals. The exception here is if you are working with a fast moving team and your launch phase is not far into the future.

Option B: Shared VMs for reduced costs

Below you can see an alternate infrastructure that demonstrates how you can achieve the same outcome while operating on fewer resources. One of many drawbacks here is that you have a single point of failure shared between staging and production for both your GKE cluster and database. If one goes, they both go. The huge benefit here is that you're saving heavily on VM costs while initially building your application.

Recommendation 📌

Use shared VMs then migrate to many VMs before launch. This approach requires that you maintain strong documentation for setting up your infrastructure. You will need to recall your experience working with cloud products to minimize the time you spend migrating closer to launching applications. By taking this approach you can save yourself hundreds of dollars each month during your pre-seed/development phase.

Coming up next...

Software Engineering Entrepreneurship » Issue 6 » Development environments to live and breathe by
Feb. 16th - 23rd
item.author: morganw

item.title: Jupyter 笔记本的格式化程序 | Formatter for Jupyter notebooks
item.description:
西德什·阿加瓦尔

发表于 2 月 18 日

如果您从事数据科学或机器学习，您可能遇到过 jupyter notebooks（.ipynb 文件）。 我在使用 jupyter notebooks 时遇到的问题是黑色格式化程序对它们不起作用。 我曾尝试使用

命令多次。 本文旨在帮助 Python Notebooks 中的代码格式化。

质量保证

因此，我们将使用一个名为 nbQA 的 python 库以及代码格式化程序，如 Black 和 isort。

安装

使用以下命令安装库：

用法

您可以将各种格式化程序与 nqba 一起使用，我将演示如何使用其中的一些。 在尝试格式化程序之前，请确保您已经安装了它们。

黑色的

使用黑色格式化笔记本，如下所示：

 $ nbqa black notebook.ipynb reformatted notebook.ipynb All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨ 1 files reformatted.

排序

同样，使用 isort 格式化笔记本：

 $ nbqa isort notebook.ipynb Fixing notebook.ipynb

格式

要格式化笔记本中的降价单元格，请使用：

 $ nbqa mdformat notebook.ipynb --nbqa-md --nbqa-diff

医生测试

要使用文档类型为 iPython 笔记本运行测试：

 $ nbqa doctest notebook.ipynb

我希望你喜欢它。 这次就这些了。

原文:

Siddhesh Agarwal

Posted on Feb 18

If you are into Data Science or Machine Learning, you have probably come across jupyter notebooks (.ipynb files). The problem I faced when using jupyter notebooks was that the black formatter didn't work on them. I had tried using the

command many times. This article is meant to help with code formatting in Python Notebooks.

nbQA

So, we will be using a python library called nbQA along with code formatters like Black and isort.

Installation

Install the library using:

Usage

You can use various formatters along with nqba and I will demonstrate how to use a few of them. before trying the formatters, make sure you have installed them already.

black

Format the notebook using black as shown below:

$ nbqa black notebook.ipynb
reformatted notebook.ipynb
All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨
1 files reformatted.

isort

Similarly, format the notebook using isort:

$ nbqa isort notebook.ipynb
Fixing notebook.ipynb

yapf 

$ nbqa yapf --in-place notebook.ipynb

autopep8 

$ nbqa autopep8 -i notebook.ipynb

mdformat

To format the markdown cells in your notebook, use:

$ nbqa mdformat notebook.ipynb --nbqa-md --nbqa-diff

doctest

To run tests for iPython notebooks using doctypes:

$ nbqa doctest notebook.ipynb

I hope you liked it. That's all for this time.

item.title: 了解 React Hooks：综合指南 | Understanding React Hooks: A Comprehensive Guide
item.description:
此功能自 React 16.8 版以来就已存在，并已成为开发人员最喜欢的工具。 在本文中，我们将深入探讨 React Hooks 是什么、它们为何必不可少以及如何有效地使用它们。

什么是反应钩子？

在引入 Hooks 之前，开发人员必须使用类组件来访问状态和生命周期方法。 这种限制使得在组件之间重用逻辑变得具有挑战性，并且经常导致复杂的类层次结构。

最常用的两个 Hook 分别是 useState 和 useEffect。

为什么 Hooks 必不可少？

钩子提供了一种优雅而直接的方式来管理功能组件的状态。 它们还通过减少所需类组件的数量来简化代码库。 借助 Hooks，开发人员可以更高效地构建复杂的应用程序并减少错误。

使用 Hooks 还可以更轻松地测试代码。 通过将状态和生命周期方法与组件分离，开发人员可以专注于测试逻辑，而不必担心组件的实现细节。

如何使用反应钩子

要在您的项目中使用 React Hooks，您必须先安装 React 16.8 或更高版本。 然后，您可以开始在功能组件中使用 useState 和 useEffect Hooks。

要使用此 Hook，您需要声明一个变量和一个将更新变量状态的函数。

 import React, { useState } from 'react'; function Example() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <div> <p>You clicked {count} times</p> <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}> Click me </button> </div> ); }

要使用此 Hook，您需要声明一个函数，该函数将在组件挂载、卸载或更新时执行。

 import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; function Example() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); useEffect(() => { document.title = `You clicked ${count} times`; }); return ( <div> <p>You clicked {count} times</p> <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}> Click me </button> </div> ); }

结论

它们允许您在功能组件中管理状态和生命周期方法，从而简化您的代码库并更容易测试您的代码。 有效地理解和使用 Hooks 将使您的开发过程更加高效和愉快。

总之，通过使用 React Hooks，您可以简化代码库、减少错误并提高开发过程的效率。 我希望这份综合指南为您提供了必要的信息，让您今天就可以在您的项目中开始使用 React Hooks。



原文:

React Hooks are a relatively new feature in the React library that allows developers to write reusable and stateful logic in functional components. This feature has been around since React version 16.8 and has since become a favorite tool among developers. In this article, we will dive into what React Hooks are, why they are essential, and how to use them effectively.

What are React Hooks?

React Hooks are functions that allow you to use React state and lifecycle methods inside functional components. Before the introduction of Hooks, developers had to use class components to access the state and lifecycle methods. This limitation made it challenging to reuse logic between components and often led to complex class hierarchies.

React Hooks are divided into two categories: State Hooks and Effect Hooks. State Hooks allow you to manage the state of a functional component, while Effect Hooks allow you to run side effects within a component. The two most commonly used Hooks are useState and useEffect, respectively.

Why are Hooks Essential?

Hooks provide an elegant and straightforward way to manage the state of a functional component. They also simplify the codebase by reducing the number of class components needed. With Hooks, developers can build complex applications more efficiently and with fewer bugs.

Using Hooks also makes it easier to test the code. By separating the state and lifecycle methods from the component, the developer can focus on testing the logic without worrying about the component's implementation details.

How to Use React Hooks

To use React Hooks in your project, you must first install React 16.8 or later. You can then start using the useState and useEffect Hooks in your functional components.

The useState Hook allows you to add state to a functional component. To use this Hook, you need to declare a variable and a function that will update the variable's state.

import React, { useState } from 'react'; function Example() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <div> <p>You clicked {count} times</p>
 <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}> Click me </button>
 </div>
 );
}

The useEffect Hook allows you to run side effects within a component. To use this Hook, you need to declare a function that will execute when the component mounts, unmounts, or updates.

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; function Example() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); useEffect(() => { document.title = `You clicked ${count} times`; }); return ( <div> <p>You clicked {count} times</p>
 <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}> Click me </button>
 </div>
 );
}

Conclusion

React Hooks are a powerful tool for building scalable and reusable applications. They allow you to manage state and lifecycle methods within functional components, simplifying your codebase and making it easier to test your code. Understanding and using Hooks effectively will make your development process more efficient and enjoyable.

In summary, by using React Hooks, you can simplify your codebase, reduce bugs, and increase the efficiency of your development process. I hope this comprehensive guide has given you the necessary information to start using React Hooks in your project today.
item.author: haszankauna

item.title: 如何将 Docker 与 Ruby on Rails 应用程序结合使用。 | How to use Docker with Ruby on Rails applications.
item.description:
粗糙的帕特尔

发表于 2 月 18 日

容器是一个轻量级、独立且可执行的软件包，其中包含运行应用程序所需的一切，包括代码、运行时、库、系统工具和设置。 一致的开发环境：通过使用 Docker 创建包含应用程序所有必要依赖项和工具的容器，您可以确保所有开发人员都在同一环境中工作。 这有助于防止因操作系统或安装的软件不同而导致的问题。

部署更简单：Docker 允许您将应用程序及其依赖项打包到单个容器中，然后可以将其部署到任何支持 Docker 的服务器。 这使您可以更轻松地在开发、暂存和生产环境之间移动您的应用程序。

改进的可扩展性：Docker 容器可以很容易地复制和水平扩展，允许您通过根据需要启动额外的容器来处理更多的流量。 这有助于提高应用程序的性能和可靠性。

简化维护：使用 Docker，您可以将您的应用程序及其依赖项与宿主系统隔离开来，这样可以更轻松地维护和更新您的应用程序，而不会影响同一台服务器上运行的其他应用程序。

总体而言，将 Docker 与 Ruby on Rails 应用程序结合使用有助于简化开发、部署和维护，同时提高应用程序的性能和可扩展性。

以下是如何将 Docker 与 Ruby on Rails 应用程序、Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 一起使用的分步示例：

第 1 步：安装 Docker
在使用 Docker 之前，您需要在系统上安装它。 您可以从官方网站下载并安装 Docker。

第 2 步：创建一个新的 Rails 应用程序假设您已经在系统上安装了 Ruby，您可以使用以下命令创建一个新的 Rails 应用程序：

$ rails new myapp

第 3 步：将 Sidekiq 添加到您的应用程序 要将 Sidekiq 与您的 Rails 应用程序一起使用，您需要将 sidekiq gem 添加到您的 Gemfile 并运行 bundle install。 您还应该使用以下命令生成一个 config/sidekiq.yml 文件：

$ bundle exec sidekiq --config config/sidekiq.yml

第 4 步：将 Redis 添加到您的应用程序
要将 Redis 与 Sidekiq 一起使用，您需要将 redis gem 添加到 Gemfile 并运行 bundle install。 您还应该在config/sidekiq.yml文件中配置 Redis。

第 5 步：将 PostgreSQL 添加到您的应用程序
要将 PostgreSQL 与 Rails 应用程序一起使用，您需要将 pg gem 添加到 Gemfile 并运行 bundle install。 您还应该更新您的config/database.yml文件以使用 PostgreSQL。

第 6 步：创建 Dockerfile 在 Rails 应用程序目录的根目录中创建一个名为 Dockerfile 的新文件。 此文件将为您的应用程序定义 Docker 映像。 下面是带有 Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 的 Rails 应用程序的 Dockerfile 示例：

 # Use an official Ruby runtime as a parent image FROM ruby:3.0.3 # Set the working directory WORKDIR /app # Install dependencies RUN apt-get update && \ apt-get install -y \ build-essential \ nodejs \ postgresql-client && \ rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* # Install gems COPY Gemfile Gemfile.lock ./ RUN gem install bundler && \ bundle install --jobs 4 # Copy the application code COPY . . # Expose ports EXPOSE 3000 # Set the entrypoint command CMD ["rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0"]

第 7 步：创建docker-compose.yml文件
在 Rails 应用程序目录的根目录中创建一个名为docker-compose.yml的新文件。 该文件将为您的应用程序定义 Docker 服务。 下面是带有 Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 的 Rails 应用程序的示例docker-compose.yml文件：

 version: "3" services: web: build: . command: bundle exec rails s -p 3000 -b '0.0.0.0' ports: - "3000:3000" depends_on: - db - redis db: image: postgres:12 environment: POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres POSTGRES_DB: myapp_development redis: image: redis:6.0

第 8 步：构建并启动 Docker 容器要为您的应用程序构建并启动 Docker 容器，请在 Rails 应用程序目录的根目录中运行以下命令：

$ docker-compose up --build

这将构建 Docker 映像并为您的应用程序、Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 启动容器。

第 9 步：访问您的应用程序
一旦 Docker 容器启动并运行，您应该能够在 Web 浏览器中通过http://localhost:3000访问您的 Rails 应用程序。 您还应该能够访问http://localhost:3000/sidekiq上的 Sidekiq Web 界面。

第 10 步：停止并删除 Docker 容器使用完应用程序后，您可以在 Rails 应用程序目录的根目录中使用以下命令停止并删除 Docker 容器：

$ docker-compose stop

这将停止并删除您的应用程序、Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 的 Docker 容器。

就是这样！ 您现在已经使用 Docker 通过 Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 运行 Ruby on Rails 应用程序。 使用 Docker，您可以轻松地在任何环境中打包和运行您的应用程序，从而轻松地根据需要部署和扩展您的应用程序。

如何在生产中做到这一点？

以下是如何使用 Docker 和 Docker Compose 将 Ruby on Rails 应用程序部署到生产环境的示例：

第 1 步：构建您的 Docker 映像

首先，您需要为 Rails 应用程序构建 Docker 映像。 在 Rails 应用程序目录的根目录中，运行以下命令：
$ docker build -t myapp .

这将基于 Rails 应用程序目录中的 Dockerfile 构建一个名为 myapp 的 Docker 镜像。

第 2 步：将 Docker 镜像推送到注册表

接下来，您需要将 Docker 映像推送到注册表，以便可以将其部署到您的生产环境。 您可以使用 Docker Hub 等公共注册表或 Amazon ECR 或 Google Container Registry 等私有注册表。

要将您的 Docker 映像推送到 Docker Hub，如果您还没有帐户，请先创建一个帐户。 然后，使用您的 Docker Hub 用户名和图像存储库的名称标记您的 Docker 图像：

$ docker tag myapp your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest

最后，将您的 Docker 镜像推送到 Docker Hub：
$ docker push your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest

第 3 步：创建生产 Docker Compose 文件

在 Rails 应用程序目录的根目录中创建一个名为docker-compose.prod.yml的新文件。 该文件将为您的生产环境定义 Docker 服务。 下面是带有 Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 的 Rails 应用程序的示例docker-compose.prod.yml文件：

 version: "3" services: web: image: your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest environment: RAILS_ENV: production DATABASE_URL: "postgres://postgres:password@db/myapp_production" REDIS_URL: "redis://redis:6379/0" ports: - "80:3000" depends_on: - db - redis restart: always db: image: postgres:12 environment: POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password POSTGRES_DB: myapp_production volumes: - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data restart: always redis: image: redis:6.0 restart: always volumes: db_data:

请注意，Web 服务的图像属性设置为 Docker Hub 上的 Docker 图像的名称。 环境属性设置 Rails 应用程序将用于连接到 PostgreSQL 和 Redis 的环境变量。 第 4 步：将您的应用程序部署到服务器

要将 Rails 应用程序部署到服务器，您需要在服务器上安装 Docker 和 Docker Compose。

安装 Docker 和 Docker Compose 后，将docker-compose.prod.yml文件复制到服务器并在文件所在的目录中运行以下命令：

$ docker-compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d
这将为您的 Rails 应用程序、Sidekiq、Redis 和 PostgreSQL 在后台启动 Docker 容器。

第 5 步：访问您的应用程序

您还应该能够通过http://your-server-ip-address/sidekiq访问 Sidekiq 网络界面

第 6 步：更新您的应用程序

要更新您的应用程序，您需要为更新后的代码构建一个新的 Docker 映像并将其推送到您的 Docker 注册表。 然后，在服务器上，您可以使用以下命令拉取新镜像并启动新容器：

$ docker pull your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest
$ docker-compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d

这将拉取新的 Docker 映像并为您的应用程序启动一个新容器，同时保持您的数据库和 Redis 数据完好无损。

第 7 步：监控您的应用程序

要在生产环境中监控 Rails 应用程序，您可以使用 New Relic 或 Scout 等工具。 这些工具为您的应用程序提供实时监控和警报，因此您可以快速识别并修复出现的任何问题。

第 8 步：扩展您的应用程序

要在生产环境中扩展 Rails 应用程序，您可以使用 Docker Compose 在负载均衡器后面运行 Rails 应用程序的多个容器。 这是一个示例docker-compose.prod.yml文件，用于运行 Rails 应用程序的多个容器：

 version: "3" services: web: image: your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest environment: RAILS_ENV: production DATABASE_URL: "postgres://postgres:password@db/myapp_production" REDIS_URL: "redis://redis:6379/0" ports: - "80:3000" depends_on: - db - redis deploy: replicas: 3 restart_policy: condition: on-failure update_config: parallelism: 2 delay: 10s resources: limits: cpus: "0.5" memory: 512M reservations: cpus: "0.25" memory: 256M db: image: postgres:12 environment: POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password POSTGRES_DB: myapp_production volumes: - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data redis: image: redis:6.0 volumes: db_data:

请注意，Web 服务有一个部署属性，该属性指定它应该使用 3 个副本运行。 资源属性设置容器的资源限制和保留。

通过此配置，您可以使用 Nginx 或 HAProxy 等负载均衡器在 Rails 应用程序的多个容器之间分配流量。

配置 Nginx 或 Apache 等反向代理，将传入的 HTTP 请求路由到运行 Rails 应用程序的 Docker 容器。 您可以将反向代理配置为侦听端口 80 或 443 并将请求转发到端口 3000，这是 Rails 应用程序的默认端口。 您还可以为安全连接配置 SSL/TLS 加密。 以下是 Rails 应用程序的 Nginx 配置文件示例：

 upstream myapp { server localhost:3000; } server { listen 80; server_name example.com; location / { proxy_pass http://myapp; proxy_set_header Host $host proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; } }

谢谢，
残酷的

当 Node.js 处理数据库查询等任务时，幕后会发生什么？ 我们将按照这段代码逐步探索它。

一旦暂停，harsh_u115 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，harsh_u115 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，harsh_u115 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 harsh_u115 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 harsh_u115 的方法：

降低 harsh_u115 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 harsh_u115 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Harsh patel

Posted on Feb 18

Docker is a platform for building, shipping, and running applications in containers. A container is a lightweight, standalone, and executable package of software that includes everything needed to run an application, including code, runtime, libraries, system tools, and settings. Docker makes it easy to package an application and all its dependencies into a single container, which can then be deployed to any environment that supports Docker.

Docker provides a way to package and distribute your application as a container, which makes it easier to manage dependencies, ensure consistent environments across different servers, and simplify deployment.

**Here are some of the benefits of using Docker with Ruby on Rails applications:

Consistent development environments: By using Docker to create a container that contains all the necessary dependencies and tools for your application, you can ensure that all developers are working in the same environment. This can help prevent issues caused by differences in operating systems or installed software.

Easier deployment: Docker allows you to package your application and its dependencies into a single container, which can then be deployed to any server that supports Docker. This makes it easier to move your application between development, staging, and production environments.

Improved scalability: Docker containers can be easily replicated and scaled horizontally, allowing you to handle more traffic by spinning up additional containers as needed. This can help improve the performance and reliability of your application.

Simplified maintenance: With Docker, you can isolate your application and its dependencies from the host system, which makes it easier to maintain and update your application without affecting other applications running on the same server.

Overall, using Docker with Ruby on Rails applications can help simplify development, deployment, and maintenance, while improving the performance and scalability of your application.

Here's a step-by-step example of how to use Docker with a Ruby on Rails application, Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL:

Step 1: Install Docker
Before you can use Docker, you need to install it on your system. You can download and install Docker from the official website.

Step 2: Create a new Rails application Assuming you already have Ruby installed on your system, you can create a new Rails application with the following command:

$ rails new myapp

Step 3: Add Sidekiq to your application To use Sidekiq with your Rails application, you need to add the sidekiq gem to your Gemfile and run bundle install. You should also generate a config/sidekiq.yml file with the following command:

$ bundle exec sidekiq --config config/sidekiq.yml

Step 4: Add Redis to your application
To use Redis with Sidekiq, you need to add the redis gem to your Gemfile and run bundle install. You should also configure Redis in your config/sidekiq.yml file.

Step 5: Add PostgreSQL to your application
To use PostgreSQL with your Rails application, you need to add the pg gem to your Gemfile and run bundle install. You should also update your config/database.yml file to use PostgreSQL.

Step 6: Create a Dockerfile Create a new file called Dockerfile in the root of your Rails application directory. This file will define the Docker image for your application. Here's an example Dockerfile for a Rails application with Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL:

# Use an official Ruby runtime as a parent image
FROM ruby:3.0.3 # Set the working directory
WORKDIR /app # Install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \ apt-get install -y \ build-essential \ nodejs \ postgresql-client && \ rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* # Install gems
COPY Gemfile Gemfile.lock ./
RUN gem install bundler && \ bundle install --jobs 4 # Copy the application code
COPY . . # Expose ports
EXPOSE 3000 # Set the entrypoint command
CMD ["rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0"]

Step 7: Create a docker-compose.yml file
Create a new file called docker-compose.yml in the root of your Rails application directory. This file will define the Docker services for your application. Here's an example docker-compose.yml file for a Rails application with Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL:

version: "3"
services: web: build: . command: bundle exec rails s -p 3000 -b '0.0.0.0' ports: - "3000:3000" depends_on: - db - redis db: image: postgres:12 environment: POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres POSTGRES_DB: myapp_development redis: image: redis:6.0

Step 8: Build and start the Docker containers To build and start the Docker containers for your application, run the following command in the root of your Rails application directory:

$ docker-compose up --build

This will build the Docker images and start the containers for your application, Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL.

Step 9: Access your application
Once the Docker containers are up and running, you should be able to access your Rails application in your web browser at http://localhost:3000. You should also be able to access the Sidekiq web interface athttp://localhost:3000/sidekiq.

Step 10: Stop and remove the Docker containers When you're finished working with your application, you can stop and remove the Docker containers with the following command in the root of your Rails application directory:

$ docker-compose stop

This will stop and remove the Docker containers for your application, Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL.

That's it! You've now used Docker to run a Ruby on Rails application with Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL. With Docker, you can easily package and run your application in any environment, making it easy to deploy and scale your application as needed.

How to do it with production?

Here's an example of how to deploy your Ruby on Rails application to a production environment using Docker and Docker Compose:

Step 1: Build your Docker image

First, you need to build your Docker image for your Rails application. In the root of your Rails application directory, run the following command:
$ docker build -t myapp .

This will build a Docker image named myapp based on the Dockerfile in your Rails application directory.

Step 2: Push your Docker image to a registry

Next, you need to push your Docker image to a registry so that it can be deployed to your production environment. You can use a public registry like Docker Hub or a private registry like Amazon ECR or Google Container Registry.

To push your Docker image to Docker Hub, first create an account if you don't have one already. Then, tag your Docker image with your Docker Hub username and the name of your image repository:

$ docker tag myapp your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest

Finally, push your Docker image to Docker Hub:
$ docker push your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest

Step 3: Create a production Docker Compose file

Create a new file called docker-compose.prod.yml in the root of your Rails application directory. This file will define the Docker services for your production environment. Here's an example docker-compose.prod.yml file for a Rails application with Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL:

version: "3"
services: web: image: your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest environment: RAILS_ENV: production DATABASE_URL: "postgres://postgres:password@db/myapp_production" REDIS_URL: "redis://redis:6379/0" ports: - "80:3000" depends_on: - db - redis restart: always db: image: postgres:12 environment: POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password POSTGRES_DB: myapp_production volumes: - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data restart: always redis: image: redis:6.0 restart: always
volumes: db_data:

Note that the image property for the web service is set to the name of your Docker image on Docker Hub. The environment properties set environment variables that your Rails application will use to connect to PostgreSQL and Redis. The ports property maps port 80 on the host to port 3000 in the container. The depends_on property specifies that the web service depends on the db and redis services. The restart property specifies that the services should be restarted if they fail.

Step 4: Deploy your application to a server

To deploy your Rails application to a server, you'll need to install Docker and Docker Compose on the server.

Once Docker and Docker Compose are installed, copy your docker-compose.prod.yml file to the server and run the following command in the directory where the file is located:

$ docker-compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d
This will start the Docker containers for your Rails application, Sidekiq, Redis, and PostgreSQL in the background.

Step 5: Access your application

Once the Docker containers are up and running, you should be able to access your Rails application in your web browser at your-server-ip-address. You should also be able to access the Sidekiq web interface at http://your-server-ip-address/sidekiq

Step 6: Update your application

To update your application, you'll need to build a new Docker image for your updated code and push it to your Docker registry. Then, on the server, you can pull the new image and start a new container using the following commands:

$ docker pull your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest
$ docker-compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d

This will pull the new Docker image and start a new container for your application, while leaving your database and Redis data intact.

Step 7: Monitor your application

To monitor your Rails application in production, you can use a tool like New Relic or Scout. These tools provide real-time monitoring and alerting for your application, so you can quickly identify and fix any issues that arise.

Step 8: Scale your application

To scale your Rails application in production, you can use Docker Compose to run multiple containers of your Rails application behind a load balancer. Here's an example docker-compose.prod.yml file for running multiple containers of your Rails application:

version: "3"
services: web: image: your-dockerhub-username/myapp:latest environment: RAILS_ENV: production DATABASE_URL: "postgres://postgres:password@db/myapp_production" REDIS_URL: "redis://redis:6379/0" ports: - "80:3000" depends_on: - db - redis deploy: replicas: 3 restart_policy: condition: on-failure update_config: parallelism: 2 delay: 10s resources: limits: cpus: "0.5" memory: 512M reservations: cpus: "0.25" memory: 256M db: image: postgres:12 environment: POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password POSTGRES_DB: myapp_production volumes: - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data redis: image: redis:6.0
volumes: db_data:

Note that the web service has a deploy property that specifies that it should be run with 3 replicas. The update_config property specifies that updates should be rolled out with a maximum parallelism of 2 and a delay of 10 seconds between updates. The resources property sets resource limits and reservations for the containers.

With this configuration, you can use a load balancer like Nginx or HAProxy to distribute traffic across the multiple containers of your Rails application.

Configure a reverse proxy like Nginx or Apache to route incoming HTTP requests to the Docker container running your Rails application. You can configure the reverse proxy to listen on port 80 or 443 and forward requests to port 3000, which is the default port for Rails applications. You can also configure SSL/TLS encryption for secure connections. Here is an example of an Nginx configuration file for a Rails application:

upstream myapp { server localhost:3000;
} server { listen 80; server_name example.com; location / { proxy_pass http://myapp; proxy_set_header Host $host proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; }
}

Thanks,
Harsh

Node.js doesn’t stop from running other operations because of Libuv, a C++ library responsible for the event loop and asynchronously handling tasks such as network requests, DNS resolution, file system operations, data encryption, etc.

What happens under the hood when Node.js works on tasks such as database queries? We will explore it by following this piece of code step by step.
